findings.description: `Services/FileService.cs` enforces two user-visible limits that the README never mentions. (1) `MaxCopyDepth = 32` is NEW to this branch: `CopyDirectory` aborts with `IOException` ("Directory copy depth limit exceeded (possible cycle)") once recursion reaches 32 levels, so a user copying a deeply nested tree gets an error they cannot explain from the docs. (2) `MaxSearchResults = 500` (pre-existing): `Search` silently stops collecting once it has 500 matches, so a broad query can return fewer results than exist with no indication. Both are behavior-affecting and belong in the README so users understand truncated results / copy failures. Add a short `## Limits` subsection documenting both, including the exact error the copy depth limit raises.
findings.fixPrompt:
Edit `README.md`. Insert a new `## Limits` section immediately after the existing `## Configuration` section (before `## Deep Linking`). Use exactly this content:

## Limits

- **Search results are capped at 500 matches.** A recursive search stops collecting once it reaches 500 entries, so very broad queries may return a truncated result set without further indication.
- **Directory copies are capped at 32 levels of nesting.** Copying a tree deeper than 32 levels aborts with an error (`IOException`: "Directory copy depth limit exceeded (possible cycle)") to guard against stack overflow on pathological or self-referential trees.

Do not modify any other section.

findings.title: Verification checklist omits `path` and `parent` from the browse response
findings.description: The Final End-to-End Verification Checklist item 5 reads: `GET /api/files/browse?path=` — returns JSON with `entries`, `folderCount`, `fileCount`, and `totalSize`. The actual response (see `Models/FileSystemModels.cs` `BrowseResultDto` and `src/api.ts` `BrowseResult`) also includes `path` (the listed folder's relative path) and `parent` (the normalized parent path, or `null` at the root). A reviewer following the checklist would think two fields are missing when the API is working correctly. Update the checklist to list the full response shape.
findings.fixPrompt: Edit `README.md`. In the `## Final End-to-End Verification Checklist` section, find item 5 on line 64: `5. **\`GET /api/files/browse?path=\`** — returns JSON with \`entries\`, \`folderCount\`, \`fileCount\`, and \`totalSize\`.` Replace it with: `5. **\`GET /api/files/browse?path=\`** — returns JSON with \`path\`, \`parent\`, \`entries\`, \`folderCount\`, \`fileCount\`, and \`totalSize\`.` Leave all other checklist items unchanged.

findings.title: Development diary has no entry for the refactor/hardening work
findings.description: `dev-diary.md` is the project's development log and currently has only two entries (harness generation; human review with embedded mode). This branch performed substantial additional work — splitting the `src/app.ts` monolith into focused modules under `src/app/`, restructuring the test suite into per-domain files, hardening `FileService` (lazy directory creation, copy-depth guard, idempotent delete, TOCTOU elimination), refactoring `FilesController` with a shared `Execute`/`ExecuteAsync` fault translator, and adding an ESLint flat config — none of which is recorded. The diary should reflect this work so the development history is accurate.
findings.fixPrompt:
Edit `dev-diary.md`. Append a new numbered entry (item 3) after the existing item 2. Use exactly this content:

3. Refactor and hardening pass: split the `src/app.ts` monolith into focused modules under `src/app/` (context, dom-builders, render-browse, render-search, render-orchestrator, toolbar-handlers) and reorganized the test suite into per-domain files. Hardened `FileService` (lazy home-directory creation via a thread-safe `Lazy`, idempotent delete without a TOCTOU check, a 32-level copy-depth guard, single-pass browse enumeration) and refactored `FilesController` to translate well-known file-system faults to `400 Bad Request` through shared `Execute`/`ExecuteAsync` helpers. Added an ESLint flat config for the TypeScript frontend and build scripts.

Do not edit the existing entries 1 or 2.

output.summary: Reviewed the full workflow changeset (branch engin/refactor-file-browser-fix-bugs-harden diverging from 6f55a97): the TS SPA was decomposed from a ~550-line src/app.ts into focused modules under src/app/ (context, dom-builders, render-browse/search, toolbar-handlers, render-orchestrator), the old src/app.test.ts monolith was split into per-domain test files plus shared src/app/test-helpers.ts and src/test-utils/mock-response.ts, and the C# FilesController/FileService were hardened (TOCTOU-safe Delete, null-parent Move guard, bounded copy depth, lazy root). tsc, eslint, vitest (333) and dotnet test (316) all pass clean with no dead code, unused imports, or leftover debug statements. The issues found are readability/maintainability: (1) a cluster of stale comments that still reference the now-deleted src/app.test.ts and describe the already-completed split/mockResponse-dedup as future work; (2) duplicated exception-translation catch blocks across the sync/async controller helpers; plus two low-severity DRY nits in the DOM error formatting and the characterization test's self-contained helpers.

findings.title: Stale comments reference deleted src/app.test.ts and frame the completed refactor/dedup as upcoming
findings.description:
This workflow deleted src/app.test.ts (the old monolith) and replaced it with per-domain test files plus shared src/app/test-helpers.ts and src/test-utils/mock-response.ts. However, numerous comments still describe app.test.ts as if it exists and describe the split/mockResponse-dedup as future/planned work, actively misleading future readers:

- src/app.ts:41-44 — the docstring says renderBrowse/renderSearch are re-exported "so src/app.test.ts's existing import ... continues to resolve without modification." app.test.ts no longer exists; the re-exports are actually consumed by src/app/render-browse.test.ts, render-search.test.ts, render-orchestration.test.ts, and start-app.test.ts.
- src/app.characterization.test.ts:5-34 — a NEW file whose entire preamble justifies its existence "alongside src/app.test.ts" (present tense) and describes "the upcoming split into src/app/* modules" as future work, even though the split is already done. Lines 33, 204, 216 repeat the "independent of app.test.ts" framing.
- src/api.test.ts:266-288 — the mockResponse helper comment block claims mockResponse is "defined at the top of this file" (false — it is imported from ./test-utils/mock-response on line 3), claims "an effectively-identical copy also lives inline in src/app.test.ts" (false — that file is deleted), and speaks of deduplication as future ("when the helper is deduplicated into a shared module ... after the helper is extracted ... this file imports it") when it has already happened. Inline comments at lines 377-378 and 391-393 repeat the same dead app.test.ts references.

These are not just historical provenance notes — several make factually wrong statements about the current code (where mockResponse is defined, which file imports what) that will confuse anyone navigating the codebase.
findings.fixPrompt:
Update the stale comments that reference the deleted src/app.test.ts and the already-completed mockResponse extraction so they describe the CURRENT state. Specifically:

1. In src/app.ts (lines ~41-44): rewrite the re-export rationale to state that renderBrowse/renderSearch are re-exported so the per-domain test files under src/app/ (e.g. render-browse.test.ts, render-search.test.ts, render-orchestration.test.ts, start-app.test.ts) can import them from ./app. Remove the reference to src/app.test.ts.

2. In src/app.characterization.test.ts (header comment lines ~5-34, plus inline notes at ~33, ~204, ~216): remove the "Why this file exists alongside src/app.test.ts" framing and the "upcoming split"/"must preserve" future tense. Reframe as: these characterization tests pin the exact observable DOM structure and render-lifecycle behavior of src/app.ts and its src/app/* modules (dom-builders, render-browse, render-search, toolbar-handlers, render-orchestrator) so structural changes are provably behavior-preserving. Do not reference app.test.ts as a current peer; if mentioning history at all, say the old monolith was already split.

3. In src/api.test.ts: (a) replace the mockResponse helper comment block (lines ~266-288) so it states that mockResponse is the shared helper imported from src/test-utils/mock-response.ts, that this suite pins its UNION semantics (parses text as JSON on the text-only branch), and drop all references to an inline copy in app.test.ts and to the dedup as future work. (b) Update the inline comments at lines ~377-378 and ~391-393 to not reference app.test.ts; e.g. the null-body case can note the delete endpoint relies on mockResponse({ status: 204, body: null }) mapping to {}, and the text-only case can note the shared helper parses text as JSON (union semantics).

Do not change any code behavior — only comments. Run npx vitest run and npx tsc --noEmit afterward to confirm nothing broke.

findings.title: Execute and ExecuteAsync duplicate the same five catch→BadRequest clauses
findings.description: Execute(Func<ActionResult>) and ExecuteAsync(Func<Task<ActionResult>>) each independently repeat the identical five catch clauses (ArgumentException, UnauthorizedAccessException, DirectoryNotFoundException, FileNotFoundException, IOException), each translating to BadRequest(new { error = ex.Message }) — ten near-identical catch blocks total. This is a DRY violation with a real drift risk: if the translatable-exception set ever changes (e.g. adding PathTooLongException by name, or removing one), an author must remember to update both methods identically or the sync and async endpoints will diverge. Notably the controller's own tests (FilesControllerTests.CaughtExceptions) enumerate the exact catch list and comment that "the extracted helper must preserve" it — indicating a single shared translator was the intended design. The two methods differ only in await body() vs body(), which is trivially unifyable.
findings.fixPrompt: In Controllers/FilesController.cs, eliminate the duplicated exception translation. Extract a single helper that maps a caught exception to a BadRequest (or null when it should propagate), e.g. `private static ActionResult? TranslateKnownException(Exception ex)` returning BadRequest(new { error = ex.Message }) for ArgumentException, UnauthorizedAccessException, DirectoryNotFoundException, FileNotFoundException, and IOException (in that order, preserving the existing catch precedence), and null otherwise. Then have both Execute and ExecuteAsync use it: Execute does `try { return body(); } catch (Exception ex) { return TranslateKnownException(ex) ?? throw; }` and ExecuteAsync does `try { return await body(); } catch (Exception ex) { return TranslateKnownException(ex) ?? throw; }`. Preserve the existing XML doc comments (including the note about why the return type is ActionResult), keep the same exception→400 mapping and message payload, and keep non-translated exceptions propagating unchanged. Run dotnet test TestProject.Tests/TestProject.Tests.csproj afterward; the existing CaughtExceptions/error-path tests in FilesControllerTests.cs must still pass (they pin this exact behavior).

findings.title: Error-to-string formatting repeated across DOM render modules
findings.description: The expression `'Error: ' + (err instanceof Error ? err.message : String(err))` is duplicated three times: twice within makeActionButton in src/app/dom-builders.ts (lines ~165 and ~168 — once for the async .catch path and once for the synchronous catch), and once in render() in src/app/render-orchestrator.ts (lines ~88-89, as a ternary feeding resultsEl.textContent). It is a small but genuine DRY violation: any change to how errors are formatted for the user (e.g. trimming, prefix change, adding a fallback) must be applied in three places. Extracting a tiny formatErrorMessage(err: unknown): string helper (e.g. into src/app/context.ts alongside the other shared utilities, or a new src/app/errors.ts) and using it in all three sites removes the drift risk.
findings.fixPrompt: Add a single shared helper that formats an unknown caught value into a user-facing error string, and use it everywhere errors are surfaced in the DOM. For example, add to src/app/context.ts (or a new src/app/errors.ts imported by the callers): `export function formatErrorMessage(err: unknown): string { return 'Error: ' + (err instanceof Error ? err.message : String(err)); }`. Then replace the three duplicated expressions: (1) the `.catch((err: unknown) => { getStatus().textContent = 'Error: ' + (err instanceof Error ? err.message : String(err)); })` in makeActionButton in src/app/dom-builders.ts with `getStatus().textContent = formatErrorMessage(err);`; (2) the synchronous `catch (err) { getStatus().textContent = 'Error: ' + ... }` in the same function with formatErrorMessage(err); (3) the ternary in render() in src/app/render-orchestrator.ts (`resultsEl.textContent = err instanceof Error ? 'Error: ' + err.message : 'Error: ' + String(err);`) with `resultsEl.textContent = formatErrorMessage(err);`. Import the helper where needed. Do not change observable output. Run npx vitest run and npx tsc --noEmit to confirm.

findings.title: Characterization test redefines mockResponse/fixtures that now live in shared modules
findings.description: src/app.characterization.test.ts is self-contained and defines its own mockResponse (lines ~37-54), fileEntry, browseResult, ISO, ISO_FMT, etc., all of which now have canonical shared implementations in src/test-utils/mock-response.ts and src/app/test-helpers.ts. The local mockResponse is subtly DIVERGENT from the shared one: its json() is `async () => (opts.body ?? {})`, whereas the shared helper is `async () => (opts.body ?? (opts.text ? JSON.parse(opts.text) : {}))`. The original justification ("self-contained so this file is independent of app.test.ts") is now moot because app.test.ts was deleted in this same workflow. Keeping a second, divergent copy of the response stub is a drift risk now that there is a dedicated mockResponse helper characterization suite in src/api.test.ts pinning the shared implementation's exact semantics.
findings.fixPrompt: Reduce duplication in src/app.characterization.test.ts. Delete the local mockResponse (and, if desired, the local fileEntry/browseResult/ISO/ISO_FMT helpers) and import them instead from the existing shared modules: mockResponse from ../test-utils/mock-response, and fileEntry/browseResult/ISO/ISO_FMT/etc. from ./test-helpers (re-exporting whatever is needed). If full consolidation is undesirable because this is intentionally an isolated characterization suite, then at minimum delete the local mockResponse and import the shared one so there is a single source of truth for the Response stub; the shared mockResponse is a strict superset of the local one (it additionally parses text-only bodies as JSON), and no existing call site in this file relies on the divergent branch (success calls pass body; error calls only read .text()), so behavior is preserved. Update the file's header comment accordingly (it must not claim independence from the deleted app.test.ts). Run npx vitest run src/app.characterization.test.ts and npx tsc --noEmit to confirm all 40 characterization tests still pass.

Then replace the three duplicated expressions: (1) the `.catch((err: unknown) => { getStatus().textContent = 'Error: ' + (err instanceof Error ? err.message : String(err)); })` in makeActionButton in src/app/dom-builders.ts with `getStatus().textContent = formatErrorMessage(err);`; (2) the synchronous `catch (err) { getStatus().textContent = 'Error: ' + ... }` in the same function with formatErrorMessage(err); (3) the ternary in render() in src/app/render-orchestrator.ts (`resultsEl.textContent = err instanceof Error ? 'Error: ' + err.message : 'Error: ' + String(err);`) with `resultsEl.textContent = formatErrorMessage(err);`. Import the helper where needed. Do not change observable output. Run npx vitest run and npx tsc --noEmit to confirm.

findings.title: Characterization test redefines mockResponse/fixtures that now live in shared modules
findings.description: src/app.characterization.test.ts is self-contained and defines its own mockResponse (lines ~37-54), fileEntry, browseResult, ISO, ISO_FMT, etc., all of which now have canonical shared implementations in src/test-utils/mock-response.ts and src/app/test-helpers.ts. The local mockResponse is subtly DIVERGENT from the shared one: its json() is `async () => (opts.body ?? {})`, whereas the shared helper is `async () => (opts.body ?? (opts.text ? JSON.parse(opts.text) : {}))`. The original justification ("self-contained so this file is independent of app.test.ts") is now moot because app.test.ts was deleted in this same workflow. Keeping a second, divergent copy of the response stub is a drift risk now that there is a dedicated mockResponse helper characterization suite in src/api.test.ts pinning the shared implementation's exact semantics.
findings.fixPrompt: Reduce duplication in src/app.characterization.test.ts. Delete the local mockResponse (and, if desired, the local fileEntry/browseResult/ISO/ISO_FMT helpers) and import them instead from the existing shared modules: mockResponse from ../test-utils/mock-response, and fileEntry/browseResult/ISO/ISO_FMT/etc. from ./test-helpers (re-exporting whatever is needed). If full consolidation is undesirable because this is intentionally an isolated characterization suite, then at minimum delete the local mockResponse and import the shared one so there is a single source of truth for the Response stub; the shared mockResponse is a strict superset of the local one (it additionally parses text-only bodies as JSON), and no existing call site in this file relies on the divergent branch (success calls pass body; error calls only read .text()), so behavior is preserved. Update the file's header comment accordingly (it must not claim independence from the deleted app.test.ts). Run npx vitest run src/app.characterization.test.ts and npx tsc --noEmit to confirm all 40 characterization tests still pass.

output.summary: Reviewed the full workflow changeset (branch engin/refactor-file-browser-fix-bugs-harden diverging from 6f55a97): the TS SPA was decomposed from a ~550-line src/app.ts into focused modules under src/app/ (context, dom-builders, render-browse/search, toolbar-handlers, render-orchestrator), the old src/app.test.ts monolith was split into per-domain test files plus shared src/app/test-helpers.ts and src/test-utils/mock-response.ts, and the C# FilesController/FileService were hardened (TOCTOU-safe Delete, null-parent Move guard, bounded copy depth, lazy root). tsc, eslint, vitest (333) and dotnet test (316) all pass clean with no dead code, unused imports, or leftover debug statements. The issues found are readability/maintainability: (1) a cluster of stale comments that still reference the now-deleted src/app.test.ts and describe the already-completed split/mockResponse-dedup as future work; (2) duplicated exception-translation catch blocks across the sync/async controller helpers; plus two low-severity DRY nits in the DOM error formatting and the characterization test's self-contained helpers.

findings.title: Stale comments reference deleted src/app.test.ts and frame the completed refactor/dedup as upcoming
findings.description:
This workflow deleted src/app.test.ts (the old monolith) and replaced it with per-domain test files plus shared src/app/test-helpers.ts and src/test-utils/mock-response.ts. However, numerous comments still describe app.test.ts as if it exists and describe the split/mockResponse-dedup as future/planned work, actively misleading future readers:

- src/app.ts:41-44 — the docstring says renderBrowse/renderSearch are re-exported "so src/app.test.ts's existing import ... continues to resolve without modification." app.test.ts no longer exists; the re-exports are actually consumed by src/app/render-browse.test.ts, render-search.test.ts, render-orchestration.test.ts, and start-app.test.ts.
- src/app.characterization.test.ts:5-34 — a NEW file whose entire preamble justifies its existence "alongside src/app.test.ts" (present tense) and describes "the upcoming split into src/app/* modules" as future work, even though the split is already done. Lines 33, 204, 216 repeat the "independent of app.test.ts" framing.
- src/api.test.ts:266-288 — the mockResponse helper comment block claims mockResponse is "defined at the top of this file" (false — it is imported from ./test-utils/mock-response on line 3), claims "an effectively-identical copy also lives inline in src/app.test.ts" (false — that file is deleted), and speaks of deduplication as future ("when the helper is deduplicated into a shared module ... after the helper is extracted ... this file imports it") when it has already happened. Inline comments at lines 377-378 and 391-393 repeat the same dead app.test.ts references.

These are not just historical provenance notes — several make factually wrong statements about the current code (where mockResponse is defined, which file imports what) that will confuse anyone navigating the codebase.
findings.fixPrompt:
Update the stale comments that reference the deleted src/app.test.ts and the already-completed mockResponse extraction so they describe the CURRENT state. Specifically:

1. In src/app.ts (lines ~41-44): rewrite the re-export rationale to state that renderBrowse/renderSearch are re-exported so the per-domain test files under src/app/ (e.g. render-browse.test.ts, render-search.test.ts, render-orchestration.test.ts, start-app.test.ts) can import them from ./app. Remove the reference to src/app.test.ts.

2. In src/app.characterization.test.ts (header comment lines ~5-34, plus inline notes at ~33, ~204, ~216): remove the "Why this file exists alongside src/app.test.ts" framing and the "upcoming split"/"must preserve" future tense. Reframe as: these characterization tests pin the exact observable DOM structure and render-lifecycle behavior of src/app.ts and its src/app/* modules (dom-builders, render-browse, render-search, toolbar-handlers, render-orchestrator) so structural changes are provably behavior-preserving. Do not reference app.test.ts as a current peer; if mentioning history at all, say the old monolith was already split.

3. In src/api.test.ts: (a) replace the mockResponse helper comment block (lines ~266-288) so it states that mockResponse is the shared helper imported from src/test-utils/mock-response.ts, that this suite pins its UNION semantics (parses text as JSON on the text-only branch), and drop all references to an inline copy in app.test.ts and to the dedup as future work. (b) Update the inline comments at lines ~377-378 and ~391-393 to not reference app.test.ts; e.g. the null-body case can note the delete endpoint relies on mockResponse({ status: 204, body: null }) mapping to {}, and the text-only case can note the shared helper parses text as JSON (union semantics).

Do not change any code behavior — only comments. Run npx vitest run and npx tsc --noEmit afterward to confirm nothing broke.

findings.title: Execute and ExecuteAsync duplicate the same five catch→BadRequest clauses
findings.description: Execute(Func<ActionResult>) and ExecuteAsync(Func<Task<ActionResult>>) each independently repeat the identical five catch clauses (ArgumentException, UnauthorizedAccessException, DirectoryNotFoundException, FileNotFoundException, IOException), each translating to BadRequest(new { error = ex.Message }) — ten near-identical catch blocks total. This is a DRY violation with a real drift risk: if the translatable-exception set ever changes (e.g. adding PathTooLongException by name, or removing one), an author must remember to update both methods identically or the sync and async endpoints will diverge. Notably the controller's own tests (FilesControllerTests.CaughtExceptions) enumerate the exact catch list and comment that "the extracted helper must preserve" it — indicating a single shared translator was the intended design. The two methods differ only in await body() vs body(), which is trivially unifyable.
findings.fixPrompt: In Controllers/FilesController.cs, eliminate the duplicated exception translation. Extract a single helper that maps a caught exception to a BadRequest (or null when it should propagate), e.g. `private static ActionResult? TranslateKnownException(Exception ex)` returning BadRequest(new { error = ex.Message }) for ArgumentException, UnauthorizedAccessException, DirectoryNotFoundException, FileNotFoundException, and IOException (in that order, preserving the existing catch precedence), and null otherwise. Then have both Execute and ExecuteAsync use it: Execute does `try { return body(); } catch (Exception ex) { return TranslateKnownException(ex) ?? throw; }` and ExecuteAsync does `try { return await body(); } catch (Exception ex) { return TranslateKnownException(ex) ?? throw; }`. Preserve the existing XML doc comments (including the note about why the return type is ActionResult), keep the same exception→400 mapping and message payload, and keep non-translated exceptions propagating unchanged. Run dotnet test TestProject.Tests/TestProject.Tests.csproj afterward; the existing CaughtExceptions/error-path tests in FilesControllerTests.cs must still pass (they pin this exact behavior).

findings.title: Error-to-string formatting repeated across DOM render modules
findings.description: The expression `'Error: ' + (err instanceof Error ? err.message : String(err))` is duplicated three times: twice within makeActionButton in src/app/dom-builders.ts (lines ~165 and ~168 — once for the async .catch path and once for the synchronous catch), and once in render() in src/app/render-orchestrator.ts (lines ~88-89, as a ternary feeding resultsEl.textContent). It is a small but genuine DRY violation: any change to how errors are formatted for the user (e.g. trimming, prefix change, adding a fallback) must be applied in three places. Extracting a tiny formatErrorMessage(err: unknown): string helper (e.g. into src/app/context.ts alongside the other shared utilities, or a new src/app/errors.ts) and using it in all three sites removes the drift risk.

For example, add to src/app/context.ts (or a new src/app/errors.ts imported by the callers): `export function formatErrorMessage(err: unknown): string { return 'Error: ' + (err instanceof Error ? err.message : String(err)); }`. Then replace the three duplicated expressions: (1) the `.catch((err: unknown) => { getStatus().textContent = 'Error: ' + (err instanceof Error ? err.message : String(err)); })` in makeActionButton in src/app/dom-builders.ts with `getStatus().textContent = formatErrorMessage(err);`; (2) the synchronous `catch (err) { getStatus().textContent = 'Error: ' + ... }` in the same function with formatErrorMessage(err); (3) the ternary in render() in src/app/render-orchestrator.ts (`resultsEl.textContent = err instanceof Error ? 'Error: ' + err.message : 'Error: ' + String(err);`) with `resultsEl.textContent = formatErrorMessage(err);`. Import the helper where needed. Do not change observable output. Run npx vitest run and npx tsc --noEmit to confirm.

This is a DRY violation with a real drift risk: if the translatable-exception set ever changes (e.g. adding PathTooLongException by name, or removing one), an author must remember to update both methods identically or the sync and async endpoints will diverge. Notably the controller's own tests (FilesControllerTests.CaughtExceptions) enumerate the exact catch list and comment that "the extracted helper must preserve" it — indicating a single shared translator was the intended design. The two methods differ only in await body() vs body(), which is trivially unifyable.
findings.fixPrompt: In Controllers/FilesController.cs, eliminate the duplicated exception translation. Extract a single helper that maps a caught exception to a BadRequest (or null when it should propagate), e.g. `private static ActionResult? TranslateKnownException(Exception ex)` returning BadRequest(new { error = ex.Message }) for ArgumentException, UnauthorizedAccessException, DirectoryNotFoundException, FileNotFoundException, and IOException (in that order, preserving the existing catch precedence), and null otherwise. Then have both Execute and ExecuteAsync use it: Execute does `try { return body(); } catch (Exception ex) { return TranslateKnownException(ex) ?? throw; }` and ExecuteAsync does `try { return await body(); } catch (Exception ex) { return TranslateKnownException(ex) ?? throw; }`. Preserve the existing XML doc comments (including the note about why the return type is ActionResult), keep the same exception→400 mapping and message payload, and keep non-translated exceptions propagating unchanged. Run dotnet test TestProject.Tests/TestProject.Tests.csproj afterward; the existing CaughtExceptions/error-path tests in FilesControllerTests.cs must still pass (they pin this exact behavior).

findings.title: Error-to-string formatting repeated across DOM render modules
findings.description: The expression `'Error: ' + (err instanceof Error ? err.message : String(err))` is duplicated three times: twice within makeActionButton in src/app/dom-builders.ts (lines ~165 and ~168 — once for the async .catch path and once for the synchronous catch), and once in render() in src/app/render-orchestrator.ts (lines ~88-89, as a ternary feeding resultsEl.textContent). It is a small but genuine DRY violation: any change to how errors are formatted for the user (e.g. trimming, prefix change, adding a fallback) must be applied in three places. Extracting a tiny formatErrorMessage(err: unknown): string helper (e.g. into src/app/context.ts alongside the other shared utilities, or a new src/app/errors.ts) and using it in all three sites removes the drift risk.
findings.fixPrompt: Add a single shared helper that formats an unknown caught value into a user-facing error string, and use it everywhere errors are surfaced in the DOM. For example, add to src/app/context.ts (or a new src/app/errors.ts imported by the callers): `export function formatErrorMessage(err: unknown): string { return 'Error: ' + (err instanceof Error ? err.message : String(err)); }`. Then replace the three duplicated expressions: (1) the `.catch((err: unknown) => { getStatus().textContent = 'Error: ' + (err instanceof Error ? err.message : String(err)); })` in makeActionButton in src/app/dom-builders.ts with `getStatus().textContent = formatErrorMessage(err);`; (2) the synchronous `catch (err) { getStatus().textContent = 'Error: ' + ... }` in the same function with formatErrorMessage(err); (3) the ternary in render() in src/app/render-orchestrator.ts (`resultsEl.textContent = err instanceof Error ? 'Error: ' + err.message : 'Error: ' + String(err);`) with `resultsEl.textContent = formatErrorMessage(err);`. Import the helper where needed. Do not change observable output. Run npx vitest run and npx tsc --noEmit to confirm.

findings.title: Characterization test redefines mockResponse/fixtures that now live in shared modules
findings.description: src/app.characterization.test.ts is self-contained and defines its own mockResponse (lines ~37-54), fileEntry, browseResult, ISO, ISO_FMT, etc., all of which now have canonical shared implementations in src/test-utils/mock-response.ts and src/app/test-helpers.ts. The local mockResponse is subtly DIVERGENT from the shared one: its json() is `async () => (opts.body ?? {})`, whereas the shared helper is `async () => (opts.body ?? (opts.text ? JSON.parse(opts.text) : {}))`. The original justification ("self-contained so this file is independent of app.test.ts") is now moot because app.test.ts was deleted in this same workflow. Keeping a second, divergent copy of the response stub is a drift risk now that there is a dedicated mockResponse helper characterization suite in src/api.test.ts pinning the shared implementation's exact semantics.
findings.fixPrompt: Reduce duplication in src/app.characterization.test.ts. Delete the local mockResponse (and, if desired, the local fileEntry/browseResult/ISO/ISO_FMT helpers) and import them instead from the existing shared modules: mockResponse from ../test-utils/mock-response, and fileEntry/browseResult/ISO/ISO_FMT/etc. from ./test-helpers (re-exporting whatever is needed). If full consolidation is undesirable because this is intentionally an isolated characterization suite, then at minimum delete the local mockResponse and import the shared one so there is a single source of truth for the Response stub; the shared mockResponse is a strict superset of the local one (it additionally parses text-only bodies as JSON), and no existing call site in this file relies on the divergent branch (success calls pass body; error calls only read .text()), so behavior is preserved. Update the file's header comment accordingly (it must not claim independence from the deleted app.test.ts). Run npx vitest run src/app.characterization.test.ts and npx tsc --noEmit to confirm all 40 characterization tests still pass.

output.summary: Reviewed the full workflow changeset (branch engin/refactor-file-browser-fix-bugs-harden diverging from 6f55a97): the TS SPA was decomposed from a ~550-line src/app.ts into focused modules under src/app/ (context, dom-builders, render-browse/search, toolbar-handlers, render-orchestrator), the old src/app.test.ts monolith was split into per-domain test files plus shared src/app/test-helpers.ts and src/test-utils/mock-response.ts, and the C# FilesController/FileService were hardened (TOCTOU-safe Delete, null-parent Move guard, bounded copy depth, lazy root). tsc, eslint, vitest (333) and dotnet test (316) all pass clean with no dead code, unused imports, or leftover debug statements. The issues found are readability/maintainability: (1) a cluster of stale comments that still reference the now-deleted src/app.test.ts and describe the already-completed split/mockResponse-dedup as future work; (2) duplicated exception-translation catch blocks across the sync/async controller helpers; plus two low-severity DRY nits in the DOM error formatting and the characterization test's self-contained helpers.

findings.title: Stale comments reference deleted src/app.test.ts and frame the completed refactor/dedup as upcoming
findings.description:
This workflow deleted src/app.test.ts (the old monolith) and replaced it with per-domain test files plus shared src/app/test-helpers.ts and src/test-utils/mock-response.ts. However, numerous comments still describe app.test.ts as if it exists and describe the split/mockResponse-dedup as future/planned work, actively misleading future readers:

- src/app.ts:41-44 — the docstring says renderBrowse/renderSearch are re-exported "so src/app.test.ts's existing import ... continues to resolve without modification." app.test.ts no longer exists; the re-exports are actually consumed by src/app/render-browse.test.ts, render-search.test.ts, render-orchestration.test.ts, and start-app.test.ts.
- src/app.characterization.test.ts:5-34 — a NEW file whose entire preamble justifies its existence "alongside src/app.test.ts" (present tense) and describes "the upcoming split into src/app/* modules" as future work, even though the split is already done. Lines 33, 204, 216 repeat the "independent of app.test.ts" framing.
- src/api.test.ts:266-288 — the mockResponse helper comment block claims mockResponse is "defined at the top of this file" (false — it is imported from ./test-utils/mock-response on line 3), claims "an effectively-identical copy also lives inline in src/app.test.ts" (false — that file is deleted), and speaks of deduplication as future ("when the helper is deduplicated into a shared module ... after the helper is extracted ... this file imports it") when it has already happened. Inline comments at lines 377-378 and 391-393 repeat the same dead app.test.ts references.

These are not just historical provenance notes — several make factually wrong statements about the current code (where mockResponse is defined, which file imports what) that will confuse anyone navigating the codebase.
findings.fixPrompt:
Update the stale comments that reference the deleted src/app.test.ts and the already-completed mockResponse extraction so they describe the CURRENT state. Specifically:

1. In src/app.ts (lines ~41-44): rewrite the re-export rationale to state that renderBrowse/renderSearch are re-exported so the per-domain test files under src/app/ (e.g. render-browse.test.ts, render-search.test.ts, render-orchestration.test.ts, start-app.test.ts) can import them from ./app. Remove the reference to src/app.test.ts.

2. In src/app.characterization.test.ts (header comment lines ~5-34, plus inline notes at ~33, ~204, ~216): remove the "Why this file exists alongside src/app.test.ts" framing and the "upcoming split"/"must preserve" future tense. Reframe as: these characterization tests pin the exact observable DOM structure and render-lifecycle behavior of src/app.ts and its src/app/* modules (dom-builders, render-browse, render-search, toolbar-handlers, render-orchestrator) so structural changes are provably behavior-preserving. Do not reference app.test.ts as a current peer; if mentioning history at all, say the old monolith was already split.

3. In src/api.test.ts: (a) replace the mockResponse helper comment block (lines ~266-288) so it states that mockResponse is the shared helper imported from src/test-utils/mock-response.ts, that this suite pins its UNION semantics (parses text as JSON on the text-only branch), and drop all references to an inline copy in app.test.ts and to the dedup as future work. (b) Update the inline comments at lines ~377-378 and ~391-393 to not reference app.test.ts; e.g. the null-body case can note the delete endpoint relies on mockResponse({ status: 204, body: null }) mapping to {}, and the text-only case can note the shared helper parses text as JSON (union semantics).

Do not change any code behavior — only comments. Run npx vitest run and npx tsc --noEmit afterward to confirm nothing broke.

output.feedback: The existing test suite in FilesControllerTests.cs comprehensively covers the Execute/ExecuteAsync refactoring target. The CaughtExceptions() MemberData provides 8 exception types (5 named base types + 3 derived subtypes) that are exercised against every endpoint (Browse, Search, Upload, Download, Delete, Move, Copy) via [Theory] tests, verifying each yields a 400 BadRequest with the exact error message. Seven separate DoesNotSwallowUnhandledExceptions tests (one per endpoint) verify non-caught exceptions propagate — covering both the sync Execute path (Browse, Search, Download, Delete, Move, Copy) and the async ExecuteAsync path (Upload). Additional tests pin the exact payload shape (AssertSingleProperty), message verbatim preservation (7 message variants including unicode, XSS, multiline), single-invocation counting, and consistent status code. The Upload-specific async propagation test intentionally asserts ThrowsAsync<InvalidOperationException> to catch any helper that fails to await or wraps in AggregateException. The tests are well-structured, independent, appropriately use FakeFileService/CountingFileService doubles without over-mocking, and have no dead/disabled/tautological tests. No additional tests are needed — these are sufficient to detect any regression from the TranslateKnownException extraction.

The implementation correctly adds a loading indicator, but the test coverage is insufficient. No tests verify that 'Loading…' text appears after clearing the container and before the fetch completes. Additionally, existing tests may be broken by the change if they check for empty innerHTML before the fetch. Need to add focused tests for the loading state and update existing tests to account for the new synchronous placeholder.

output.feedback: The duplicate catch→BadRequest DRY violation is fully resolved. A single private static `TranslateKnownException(Exception)` helper now owns the exception→BadRequest mapping, and both `Execute` and `ExecuteAsync` delegate to it via a single `catch (Exception ex)` that returns the translated result or rethrows with `throw;` (preserving the stack trace). The five-type check order (ArgumentException, UnauthorizedAccessException, DirectoryNotFoundException, FileNotFoundException, IOException) matches the original catch precedence exactly, and inheritance-based subtype matching is retained (verified by the CaughtExceptions tests, which include ArgumentNullException/ArgumentOutOfRangeException/PathTooLongException). The payload (`new { error = ex.Message }` wrapped in BadRequestObjectResult) and the propagation of non-translated exceptions are behaviorally identical to the pre-refactor code. XML doc comments are preserved — including the `remarks` note on why the return type is `ActionResult` — and the new helper is documented with an explicit note that it is the single source of truth for both call sites. The CaughtExceptions-pinned error-path tests all pass (8/8). No issues found with this change.

The fix is correctly implemented and tested. The production code change (src/app.ts) adds a single line: `searchInput.setAttribute('aria-label', 'Search files');` which provides a stable accessible name for the search input. The placeholder is preserved as a hint. The 6 new tests in start-app.test.ts are well-designed and cover: (1) aria-label presence and non-empty value, (2) exact 'Search files' value, (3) persistence after user typing (the core issue — placeholders disappear when typed into), (4) placeholder preservation alongside aria-label, (5) selector compatibility (existing input[type="text"] selectors still match), and (6) attribute survival during dialog open/close widget move. All 339 tests pass across all 12 test files. No out-of-scope changes remain.

output.feedback: The test suite for the security fix is well-written, comprehensive, and correctly structured. The two new test files (FilesControllerAuthorizationTests.cs and ProgramSecurityWiringTests.cs) plus the pre-existing ReadmeDocumentationTests.cs provide thorough characterization of the expected security posture. All 34 security-related test cases (across the three files) are currently failing as expected, since the production code (FilesController.cs and Program.cs) has not yet been modified to add [Authorize], authentication/authorization middleware, or access-control documentation. This is correct TDD behavior: the tests define the contract the fix must satisfy. The tests follow project conventions (reflection for attribute checks, regex-based source parsing for Program.cs structure), are independent, have clear descriptive names, and cover edge cases such as guard against look-alike [Authorize] attributes, ensure exactly one class-level attribute, verify no [AllowAnonymous] opt-out, and filter UnauthorizedAccessException references from the XML doc check. No tautological, dead, or disabled tests were found.

The loading indicator tests in render-orchestration.test.ts are comprehensive, well-structured, and correctly pin the expected contract. The six tests cover: (1) synchronous appearance before fetch resolves on initial render, (2) browse fetch in-flight, (3) search fetch in-flight, (4) stale table clearing + placeholder in synchronous window, (5) error replacement of placeholder, and (6) placeholder reappearance on navigation. All tests follow existing patterns, use appropriate mocking (holdBrowseFetch/holdSearchFetch), and have clear descriptions. No tautological, dead, or duplicate tests exist. The existing render orchestration and lifecycle tests all use `await flush()` before asserting on results content, so they are compatible with the loading placeholder being present transiently. One minor observation: the production code in render-orchestrator.ts does not yet contain the `resultsEl.textContent = 'Loading…'` line that these tests expect, causing the 6 loading indicator tests to fail. This is expected—the tests were added in anticipation of the implementation. Once the single-line implementation change is applied, all tests should pass.

output.feedback:
The fix described in the task instructions was NOT implemented. `git diff --stat` shows that `Services/FileService.cs` and `Controllers/FilesController.cs` are completely unmodified (not present in the diff). Only `README.md`, `TestProject.Tests/Services/FileServiceTests.cs` (lazy-init characterization tests only), and an unrelated frontend test were changed.

The critical path-traversal vulnerability is fully present in the code on disk:

1. `Services/FileService.cs` `UploadAsync` still does `var path = Path.Combine(dir, file.FileName);` — the client-controlled `file.FileName` is used verbatim as a path component with no `Path.GetFileName` leafing, no invalid-char check, no containment re-check, and no `ResolveChildPath` helper.

2. `Controllers/FilesController.cs` `Upload` still does `var resultPath = string.IsNullOrEmpty(dir) ? file.FileName : $"{dir}/{file.FileName}";` — both branches echo the unsanitized filename into the response path.

3. The requested regression tests (uploading with filename `../../escape.txt`, an absolute/rooted filename, asserting `ArgumentException` and that no file is created at `Path.Combine(root, "..", "escape.txt")`) are entirely absent. The only changes to `FileServiceTests.cs` are unrelated lazy-initialization characterization tests.

Build passes and all 322 tests pass — but only because nothing was changed to enforce the security constraint. A malicious upload with `file.FileName = "../../escape.txt"` or an absolute path will still write anywhere on disk reachable from the home root.

This is a straight non-implementation of the fix and must be redone. The fix agent appears to have produced no code changes to the two target production files.

issues.description: `UploadAsync` (lines 199-206 on disk) still uses `Path.Combine(dir, file.FileName)` with the unsanitized client-controlled filename. Required: (a) `var leafName = Path.GetFileName(file.FileName);` to strip leading dirs / `..` / drive roots / absolute paths; (b) reject empty/whitespace or invalid chars via `leafName.IndexOfAny(Path.GetInvalidFileNameChars()) >= 0` throwing `ArgumentException("Invalid file name")`; (c) `var path = Path.GetFullPath(Path.Combine(dir, leafName));` with the SAME containment check as `SafeResolve` (`path == Root` or `path.StartsWith(Root + Path.DirectorySeparatorChar, StringComparison.Ordinal)`), throwing `ArgumentException("Invalid file name")` on failure; (d) only then `File.Create`/`CopyToAsync`. The suggested `ResolveChildPath(dir, fileName)` helper to hold (a)-(c) was not added. None of this is present.

`Upload` response path (line 77 on disk) still uses raw `file.FileName` in both branches: `var resultPath = string.IsNullOrEmpty(dir) ? file.FileName : $"{dir}/{file.FileName}";`. Required: replace `file.FileName` with `System.IO.Path.GetFileName(file.FileName ?? string.Empty)` in both branches so the echoed response path uses the sanitized leaf, consistent with the server-side write.

The required regression tests are missing. No test asserts `UploadAsync("", FormFileFactory.CreateFormFile("../../escape.txt", "x"))` throws `ArgumentException`; no test asserts an absolute/rooted filename throws `ArgumentException`; no test asserts `File.Exists(Path.Combine(root, "..", "escape.txt"))` is false after such an upload. The changes present in the diff are unrelated lazy-initialization characterization tests. The fix instructions explicitly required these regression tests.

The fix described in the task instructions was NOT implemented. `git diff --stat` shows that `Services/FileService.cs` and `Controllers/FilesController.cs` are completely unmodified (not present in the diff). Only `README.md`, `TestProject.Tests/Services/FileServiceTests.cs` (lazy-init characterization tests only), and an unrelated frontend test were changed.

The critical path-traversal vulnerability is fully present in the code on disk:

1. `Services/FileService.cs` `UploadAsync` still does `var path = Path.Combine(dir, file.FileName);` — the client-controlled `file.FileName` is used verbatim as a path component with no `Path.GetFileName` leafing, no invalid-char check, no containment re-check, and no `ResolveChildPath` helper.

2. `Controllers/FilesController.cs` `Upload` still does `var resultPath = string.IsNullOrEmpty(dir) ? file.FileName : $"{dir}/{file.FileName}";` — both branches echo the unsanitized filename into the response path.

3. The requested regression tests (uploading with filename `../../escape.txt`, an absolute/rooted filename, asserting `ArgumentException` and that no file is created at `Path.Combine(root, "..", "escape.txt")`) are entirely absent. The only changes to `FileServiceTests.cs` are unrelated lazy-initialization characterization tests.

output.feedback: The fix for the upload control keyboard inaccessibility (WCAG 2.1.1) has NOT been implemented yet, and the required test coverage does not exist. The current code still has `uploadInput.hidden = true` (src/app.ts:83), `src/app.css` lacks the `.visually-hidden` and `.toolbar label.btn:focus-within` rules, and the dedicated test file `src/app/upload-a11y.test.ts` does not exist on disk. The existing `start-app.test.ts` tests at lines 110-119 and 448-460 actively PIN the buggy behavior (asserting `hasAttribute('hidden') === true` and `uploadInput.className === ''`). These tests must be updated to match the fixed behavior before the fix can be applied. The `app.css.test.ts` only has 2 dialog-visibility tests and lacks the upload accessibility CSS contract tests. All 333 tests currently pass because code and tests are consistent in the buggy state — no test guards against this regression. The fix requires: (1) removing `uploadInput.hidden = true` and adding `visually-hidden` class + `aria-label` in src/app.ts, (2) adding `.visually-hidden` sr-only CSS rule and `.toolbar label.btn:focus-within` focus-ring rule in src/app.css, (3) creating `src/app/upload-a11y.test.ts` with keyboard accessibility tests, (4) updating `src/app/start-app.test.ts` upload control assertions, and (5) expanding `src/app/css.test.ts` with the `.visually-hidden` and `:focus-within` CSS contract tests.

issues.description: Dedicated keyboard accessibility test file does not exist. This file should contain tests that the upload input does NOT carry the `hidden` attribute, IS visually hidden via the `visually-hidden` sr-only class, has `aria-label="Upload files"`, is not given `tabindex="-1"`, has no `aria-hidden`, is a descendant of the `label.btn` Upload affordance, and retains `type=file` and `multiple`. Without this file, there is no focused regression guard for the WCAG 2.1.1 (Keyboard) contract.

Test at line 110 ('renders an upload control: label.btn wrapping a hidden, multiple file input') and characterization test at line 448 ('hides the file input via the `hidden` HTML attribute') both assert the BUGGY behavior: `uploadInput.hasAttribute('hidden')` === true, `uploadInput.hidden` === true, and `uploadInput.className` === ''. After the fix, these assertions would FAIL. They must be updated to assert the fixed behavior: `hasAttribute('hidden')` === false, `className` contains 'visually-hidden', and `aria-label` is 'Upload files'. The test description at line 110 should also change from 'wrapping a hidden' to 'wrapping a visually-hidden'.

Missing CSS contract tests for the upload accessibility fix. The file currently has only 2 tests for the native <dialog> visibility contract. It needs additional tests to assert: (1) a `.visually-hidden` rule exists using the sr-only clip pattern (position:absolute, 1x1 box, clip, overflow:hidden, white-space:nowrap) and does NOT use `display:none`; (2) a `.toolbar label.btn:focus-within` rule exists and applies a visible focus indicator (non-none outline/border/box-shadow/background). These tests pin the CSS half of the keyboard-accessibility contract so deleting the rule or switching to display:none would be caught.

Missing `.visually-hidden` sr-only CSS rule and `.toolbar label.btn:focus-within` focus-ring rule. The `.visually-hidden` rule must use the standard clip-to-1x1 pattern (position:absolute; width:1px; height:1px; padding:0; margin:-1px; overflow:hidden; clip:rect(0,0,0,0); white-space:nowrap; border:0) and must NOT use display:none. The `.toolbar label.btn:focus-within` rule must apply a visible focus indicator so sighted keyboard users can see focus land on the Upload button.

The upload input fix has NOT been applied. Line 83 still has `uploadInput.hidden = true`. The fix requires removing this line, adding `uploadInput.className = 'visually-hidden'` and `uploadInput.setAttribute('aria-label', 'Upload files')` instead.

Test at line 110 ('renders an upload control: label.btn wrapping a hidden, multiple file input') and characterization test at line 448 ('hides the file input via the `hidden` HTML attribute') both assert the BUGGY behavior: `uploadInput.hasAttribute('hidden')` === true, `uploadInput.hidden` === true, and `uploadInput.className` === ''. After the fix, these assertions would FAIL. They must be updated to assert the fixed behavior: `hasAttribute('hidden')` === false, `className` contains 'visually-hidden', and `aria-label` is 'Upload files'. The test description at line 110 should also change from 'wrapping a hidden' to 'wrapping a visually-hidden'.

Missing CSS contract tests for the upload accessibility fix. The file currently has only 2 tests for the native <dialog> visibility contract. It needs additional tests to assert: (1) a `.visually-hidden` rule exists using the sr-only clip pattern (position:absolute, 1x1 box, clip, overflow:hidden, white-space:nowrap) and does NOT use `display:none`; (2) a `.toolbar label.btn:focus-within` rule exists and applies a visible focus indicator (non-none outline/border/box-shadow/background). These tests pin the CSS half of the keyboard-accessibility contract so deleting the rule or switching to display:none would be caught.

Missing `.visually-hidden` sr-only CSS rule and `.toolbar label.btn:focus-within` focus-ring rule. The `.visually-hidden` rule must use the standard clip-to-1x1 pattern (position:absolute; width:1px; height:1px; padding:0; margin:-1px; overflow:hidden; clip:rect(0,0,0,0); white-space:nowrap; border:0) and must NOT use display:none. The `.toolbar label.btn:focus-within` rule must apply a visible focus indicator so sighted keyboard users can see focus land on the Upload button.

The upload input fix has NOT been applied. Line 83 still has `uploadInput.hidden = true`. The fix requires removing this line, adding `uploadInput.className = 'visually-hidden'` and `uploadInput.setAttribute('aria-label', 'Upload files')` instead.

output.feedback: The core fix is NOT present in the working tree. I verified `src/app.ts` directly: the search input (lines 71-73) still reads `searchInput.type = 'text'; searchInput.placeholder = 'Search...';` with no `aria-label`, `title`, or associated `<label>` afterward. `git diff HEAD -- src/app.ts` shows the ONLY change to that file is a doc-comment rewording (lines 38-44, the re-export explanation); the required line `searchInput.setAttribute('aria-label', 'Search files');` shown in the task description's embedded `src/app.ts` is absent from the actual file. A grep for `aria-label` in `src/app.ts` returns only line 124 (the Close button's pre-existing baseline label), and a grep for `Search files` returns nothing. This means the finding is unresolved: the search input still exposes only a placeholder, which is not an accessible name. The task description's embedded code shows the line present, but the filesystem does not match it — the implementation is incomplete against the task. Separately, the approved characterization tests from Attempts 1 & 2 (the `search input accessible name` describe block in `src/app/start-app.test.ts`) are also gone: `git diff --stat HEAD -- src/app/start-app.test.ts` is empty, so the file is unmodified vs HEAD. The out-of-scope loading-indicator test bundles flagged in Attempt 2 (`render-orchestration.test.ts` loading block; the `Loading…` assertions in `app.characterization.test.ts`) appear reverted (render-orchestration.test.ts is no longer modified), which is correct. However, the worktree is now contaminated with an entirely unrelated task's changes (access-control/authorization hardening): `Program.cs`, `Controllers/FilesController.cs`, the README "Access Control" section, `TestProject.Tests/Services/FileServiceTests.cs`, `src/api.test.ts`, `src/app/render-orchestrator.ts`, plus untracked C# files (`ProgramSecurityWiringTests.cs`, `ReadmeDocumentationTests.cs`, `SourceFileLoader.cs`). None of these relate to the search-input accessibility fix and violate the "make no other changes" instruction. To resolve: re-apply exactly one line to `src/app.ts` — `searchInput.setAttribute('aria-label', 'Search files');` — immediately after `searchInput.placeholder = 'Search...';`, restore the approved `search input accessible name` tests in `start-app.test.ts`, and exclude the unrelated access-control/C# changes from this changeset (confirm `src/app.characterization.test.ts` does not reintroduce the reverted `Loading…` assertions).

issues.description: The required accessible-name fix is missing. `git diff HEAD -- src/app.ts` shows only a doc-comment change; the line `searchInput.setAttribute('aria-label', 'Search files');` (present in the task description's embedded code) is NOT in the actual file. The search input at lines 71-73 still has only `type='text'` and `placeholder='Search...'` and no accessible name, so the UI/UX finding is unresolved. Add the `aria-label` line immediately after the placeholder assignment and keep the existing placeholder as a hint.

The `search input accessible name` describe block (6 tests that Attempts 1 and 2 approved and asked to keep) is absent — the file is unmodified vs HEAD (`git diff --stat` empty). These characterization tests should accompany the re-applied fix.

The loading-indicator fix is complete, minimal, and matches the task instructions exactly. `resultsEl.textContent = 'Loading…'` is placed immediately after `resultsEl.innerHTML = ''` and before the try/await, so the results panel is never blank while a fetch is in flight. The race-guard token logic and the intentional non-clearing of breadcrumb/status are preserved. The placeholder is naturally replaced on success (renderBrowse/renderSearch clear and rebuild the element) and on error (the catch branch reassigns textContent), so no extra cleanup is required, exactly as specified. The docstring and an inline comment were updated to document the new placeholder. The prior review feedback (insufficient test coverage) is fully resolved: a dedicated `loading placeholder` describe block in render-orchestration.test.ts adds 4 focused tests (placeholder on initial fetch, placeholder on search fetch, replaced by result table on resolve, replaced by error message on failure), all passing; the characterization suite also passes, confirming existing tests were not broken. NOTE (out of scope / WONTFIX): the wider suite shows 11 failing tests, but every failure concerns the upload control (aria-label, .visually-hidden CSS, hidden attribute, :focus-within) in upload-a11y.test.ts, app.css.test.ts, and start-app.test.ts. I verified via a git stash that these failures exist independent of this change — they are pre-existing, caused by separate incomplete upload-a11y/CSS work on the branch, and have no connection to render() or the results element. They are outside the scope of this loading-indicator task and should not block it.

The fix has NOT been applied and the required tests have NOT been created. All 333 tests pass only because code and tests are consistently describing the BUGGY behavior — no test guards against this WCAG 2.1.1 regression. Specifically: (1) src/app.ts line 87 still has `uploadInput.hidden = true` — the fix (remove hidden, add visually-hidden class + aria-label) was never applied; (2) src/app/upload-a11y.test.ts does not exist — the dedicated keyboard-accessibility test file was never created; (3) src/app.css has zero .visually-hidden or :focus-within rules — the sr-only CSS rule and focus-ring rule were never added; (4) src/app.css.test.ts is still the original 66-line file with only 2 dialog-visibility tests — the upload accessibility CSS contract tests were never added; (5) src/app/start-app.test.ts lines 117, 511-512, 516 still assert the buggy behavior (hasAttribute('hidden')===true, uploadInput.hidden===true, uploadInput.className===''). Every item from the Attempt 1 review feedback remains unresolved.

issues.description: Line 87 still has `uploadInput.hidden = true`. The fix requires removing this line and instead adding `uploadInput.className = 'visually-hidden'` and `uploadInput.setAttribute('aria-label', 'Upload files')`. This is the core production-code change that fixes the WCAG 2.1.1 failure.

Missing `.visually-hidden` sr-only CSS rule (position:absolute; width:1px; height:1px; padding:0; margin:-1px; overflow:hidden; clip:rect(0,0,0,0); white-space:nowrap; border:0) and missing `.toolbar label.btn:focus-within` focus-ring rule. Without these CSS rules, the visually-hidden input has no visual treatment and the Upload button shows no focus indicator when the keyboard focuses the input.

Dedicated keyboard-accessibility test file does not exist. It should test: input has no `hidden` attribute, input has `visually-hidden` class, input has `aria-label='Upload files'`, no tabindex=-1, no aria-hidden, input is inside the label.btn Upload affordance, and type=file + multiple are preserved.

Still the original 2-test dialog-visibility file (66 lines). Needs upload accessibility CSS contract tests: (1) .visually-hidden rule exists using sr-only clip pattern, does NOT use display:none; (2) .toolbar label.btn:focus-within rule exists and applies a visible focus indicator (non-none outline/border/box-shadow/background).

Lines 117, 511, 512, 516 still assert the buggy behavior: `hasAttribute('hidden') === true`, `uploadInput.hidden === true`, `uploadInput.className === ''`. After the fix these must assert: `hasAttribute('hidden') === false`, `className` contains `visually-hidden`, `getAttribute('aria-label')` is `'Upload files'`. The test description at line 110 ('wrapping a hidden') should also be updated to ('wrapping a visually-hidden').

output.feedback: Verified against the live worktree, and all prior feedback is now resolved. The core fix is present in src/app.ts: `searchInput.setAttribute('aria-label', 'Search files');` is added immediately after `searchInput.placeholder = 'Search...';` (line 77), with a clear inline comment explaining why a placeholder is not an accessible name. The placeholder is preserved as a hint, the input type remains 'text', and no visible <label>/structural change was made, so the `input[type="text"]` selectors used by render-search.test.ts and toolbar-handlers.test.ts continue to match (confirmed: those suites pass, 5 + 11 tests). The approved `search input accessible name` describe block (6 tests in src/app/start-app.test.ts) is present and passing, covering aria-label presence/value, persistence after typing, placeholder preservation, single-text-input selector compatibility, and widget-move invariance. I ran `npx vitest run src/app/start-app.test.ts src/app/render-search.test.ts src/app/toolbar-handlers.test.ts` — 3 files, 51 tests, all passing. Scope is now clean: `git status --porcelain` shows ONLY `src/app.ts` and `src/app/start-app.test.ts` modified; the out-of-scope loading-indicator bundles (the `loading indicator while results are being fetched` block in render-orchestration.test.ts and the `Loading…` assertions in app.characterization.test.ts — both grep to 0 matches) and the unrelated access-control/authorization contamination flagged in Attempt 3 (Program.cs, Controllers/FilesController.cs, README 'Access Control', FileServiceTests.cs, api.test.ts, render-orchestrator.ts, and the untracked C# files) are all reverted. The only non-fix change remaining is the acceptable doc-comment touch-up about the re-export rationale in src/app.ts. The implementation matches the task exactly and introduces no behavior change beyond adding the accessible name.

The critical path-traversal fix has STILL not been applied. `git status` confirms the working tree is nearly clean — only `src/app.ts` and `src/app/start-app.test.ts` (unrelated frontend files) are modified. Both target production files (`Services/FileService.cs` and `Controllers/FilesController.cs`) show zero changes, and the required regression tests are absent from `FileServiceTests.cs`.

Direct on-disk verification:
- `Services/FileService.cs` line 203: `var path = Path.Combine(dir, file.FileName);` — the client-controlled `file.FileName` is used verbatim. No `Path.GetFileName` leafing, no invalid-char rejection, no containment re-check, no `ResolveChildPath` helper.
- `Controllers/FilesController.cs` line 77: `var resultPath = string.IsNullOrEmpty(dir) ? file.FileName : $"{dir}/{file.FileName}";` — both branches echo the unsanitized filename.
- `FileServiceTests.cs`: only 3 basic UploadAsync tests exist (lines 701, 713, 725). None of the required path-traversal regression tests are present — no `../../escape.txt` filename test, no absolute/rooted filename test, no assertion that `File.Exists(Path.Combine(root, "..", "escape.txt"))` is false, no invalid-character test, no bare `..` test.

A malicious upload with `file.FileName = "../../escape.txt"` or an absolute path will write anywhere on disk reachable from the home root. This is the exact same non-implementation state identified in Attempt 1.

issues.description: `UploadAsync` (lines 198-206 on disk) still uses `var path = Path.Combine(dir, file.FileName);` with the unsanitized client-controlled filename. Per fix instructions: (1) derive `var leafName = Path.GetFileName(file.FileName);`; (2) reject empty/invalid via `if (string.IsNullOrWhiteSpace(leafName) || leafName.IndexOfAny(Path.GetInvalidFileNameChars()) >= 0) throw new ArgumentException("Invalid file name");`; (3) compute `var path = Path.GetFullPath(Path.Combine(dir, leafName));` and re-apply the SAME containment check as `SafeResolve` (`path == Root` or `path.StartsWith(Root + Path.DirectorySeparatorChar, StringComparison.Ordinal)`), throwing `ArgumentException("Invalid file name")` on failure; (4) only then `File.Create`/`CopyToAsync`. Add the optional `ResolveChildPath(dir, fileName)` private helper to encapsulate (1)-(3). None of this exists in the file.

`Upload` response path (line 77 on disk) still uses raw `file.FileName` in both branches: `var resultPath = string.IsNullOrEmpty(dir) ? file.FileName : $"{dir}/{file.FileName}";`. Required: replace `file.FileName` with `System.IO.Path.GetFileName(file.FileName ?? string.Empty)` in both branches so the echoed response uses the sanitized leaf, consistent with the server-side write.

The required regression tests are entirely missing. No test asserts `UploadAsync("", FormFileFactory.CreateFormFile("../../escape.txt", "x"))` prevents a file from being created at `Path.Combine(root, "..", "escape.txt")`. No test asserts an absolute/rooted filename is rejected. No test for invalid characters or bare `..` in the filename. Only 3 basic UploadAsync tests exist (lines 701-730); none cover the filename-sanitization security contract.

issues.description: `UploadAsync` (lines 198-206 on disk) still uses `var path = Path.Combine(dir, file.FileName);` with the unsanitized client-controlled filename. Per fix instructions: (1) derive `var leafName = Path.GetFileName(file.FileName);`; (2) reject empty/invalid via `if (string.IsNullOrWhiteSpace(leafName) || leafName.IndexOfAny(Path.GetInvalidFileNameChars()) >= 0) throw new ArgumentException("Invalid file name");`; (3) compute `var path = Path.GetFullPath(Path.Combine(dir, leafName));` and re-apply the SAME containment check as `SafeResolve` (`path == Root` or `path.StartsWith(Root + Path.DirectorySeparatorChar, StringComparison.Ordinal)`), throwing `ArgumentException("Invalid file name")` on failure; (4) only then `File.Create`/`CopyToAsync`. Add the optional `ResolveChildPath(dir, fileName)` private helper to encapsulate (1)-(3). None of this exists in the file.

`Upload` response path (line 77 on disk) still uses raw `file.FileName` in both branches: `var resultPath = string.IsNullOrEmpty(dir) ? file.FileName : $"{dir}/{file.FileName}";`. Required: replace `file.FileName` with `System.IO.Path.GetFileName(file.FileName ?? string.Empty)` in both branches so the echoed response uses the sanitized leaf, consistent with the server-side write.

The required regression tests are entirely missing. No test asserts `UploadAsync("", FormFileFactory.CreateFormFile("../../escape.txt", "x"))` prevents a file from being created at `Path.Combine(root, "..", "escape.txt")`. No test asserts an absolute/rooted filename is rejected. No test for invalid characters or bare `..` in the filename. Only 3 basic UploadAsync tests exist (lines 701-730); none cover the filename-sanitization security contract.

output.feedback:
The path-traversal / arbitrary file-write fix is now fully implemented and committed (commit 9199b7e). All three components from the fix instructions are present and verified on disk.

1. **Services/FileService.cs — UploadAsync (lines 197-211)**: The vulnerable `Path.Combine(dir, file.FileName)` is replaced with `ResolveChildPath(dir, file.FileName)`. The new private `ResolveChildPath` helper (lines 341-360) implements exactly the four required steps:
   - Step 1: `var leafName = Path.GetFileName(fileName);` strips leading dirs, `..` segments, drive roots, and absolute paths.
   - Step 2: `if (string.IsNullOrWhiteSpace(leafName) || leafName.IndexOfAny(Path.GetInvalidFileNameChars()) >= 0) throw new ArgumentException("Invalid file name");`
   - Step 3: `var path = Path.GetFullPath(Path.Combine(dir, leafName));` followed by the SAME containment predicate as `SafeResolve` — `string.Equals(path, root, StringComparison.Ordinal)` / `path.StartsWith(root + Path.DirectorySeparatorChar, StringComparison.Ordinal)` — throwing `ArgumentException("Invalid file name")` on failure.
   - Step 4: only then `File.Create` / `CopyToAsync`.
   The containment predicate is byte-for-byte identical to `SafeResolve`'s (verified by diffing both blocks). The helper is well-documented with a docstring explaining the defense-in-depth rationale.

2. **Controllers/FilesController.cs — Upload (lines 90-91)**: Both branches now use `var leaf = System.IO.Path.GetFileName(file.FileName ?? string.Empty);` and `var resultPath = string.IsNullOrEmpty(dir) ? leaf : $"{dir}/{leaf}";`. The null-coalescing `?? string.Empty` guards against a null `FileName`.

3. **TestProject.Tests/Services/FileServiceTests.cs**: Seven regression tests added (lines 734-895), all directly targeting the security finding:
   - `UploadAsync_FileNameWithParentTraversal_DoesNotWriteOutsideRoot` — `../escape.txt`, asserts `File.Exists(Path.Combine(root, "..", "escape.txt"))` is false.
   - `UploadAsync_FileNameWithMultipleParentTraversal_DoesNotWriteOutsideRoot` — `../../escape.txt` (the exact example from the finding), with explicit cleanup since the escape target is outside the temp content root.
   - `UploadAsync_AbsoluteFileName_DoesNotWriteOutsideRoot` — absolute/rooted filename, asserts no file at the absolute target.
   - `UploadAsync_EmptyOrWhitespaceFileName_ThrowsArgumentException` — empty and whitespace.
   - `UploadAsync_FileNameWithInvalidCharacters_ThrowsArgumentException` — NUL byte.
   - `UploadAsync_ParentReferenceFileName_ThrowsArgumentException` — bare `..`.
   - `UploadAsync_FileNameWithDirectoryComponent_StripsToLeafAndWritesUnderRoot` — positive test: `subdir/report.txt` is sanitized to `report.txt` under root.

All 14 UploadAsync tests pass (including the 7 new ones), and all 114 FileServiceTests pass. The fix is minimal, targeted, and follows existing code conventions (private helper with XML doc, same containment predicate as SafeResolve, same ArgumentException error pattern). No scope expansion, no dead code, no logic errors.

All 14 UploadAsync tests pass (including the 7 new ones), and all 114 FileServiceTests pass. The fix is minimal, targeted, and follows existing code conventions (private helper with XML doc, same containment predicate as SafeResolve, same ArgumentException error pattern). No scope expansion, no dead code, no logic errors.

The path-traversal / arbitrary file-write fix is now fully implemented and committed (commit 9199b7e). All three components from the fix instructions are present and verified on disk.

1. **Services/FileService.cs — UploadAsync (lines 197-211)**: The vulnerable `Path.Combine(dir, file.FileName)` is replaced with `ResolveChildPath(dir, file.FileName)`. The new private `ResolveChildPath` helper (lines 341-360) implements exactly the four required steps:
   - Step 1: `var leafName = Path.GetFileName(fileName);` strips leading dirs, `..` segments, drive roots, and absolute paths.
   - Step 2: `if (string.IsNullOrWhiteSpace(leafName) || leafName.IndexOfAny(Path.GetInvalidFileNameChars()) >= 0) throw new ArgumentException("Invalid file name");`
   - Step 3: `var path = Path.GetFullPath(Path.Combine(dir, leafName));` followed by the SAME containment predicate as `SafeResolve` — `string.Equals(path, root, StringComparison.Ordinal)` / `path.StartsWith(root + Path.DirectorySeparatorChar, StringComparison.Ordinal)` — throwing `ArgumentException("Invalid file name")` on failure.
   - Step 4: only then `File.Create` / `CopyToAsync`.
   The containment predicate is byte-for-byte identical to `SafeResolve`'s (verified by diffing both blocks). The helper is well-documented with a docstring explaining the defense-in-depth rationale.

2. **Controllers/FilesController.cs — Upload (lines 90-91)**: Both branches now use `var leaf = System.IO.Path.GetFileName(file.FileName ?? string.Empty);` and `var resultPath = string.IsNullOrEmpty(dir) ? leaf : $"{dir}/{leaf}";`. The null-coalescing `?? string.Empty` guards against a null `FileName`.

3. **TestProject.Tests/Services/FileServiceTests.cs**: Seven regression tests added (lines 734-895), all directly targeting the security finding:
   - `UploadAsync_FileNameWithParentTraversal_DoesNotWriteOutsideRoot` — `../escape.txt`, asserts `File.Exists(Path.Combine(root, "..", "escape.txt"))` is false.
   - `UploadAsync_FileNameWithMultipleParentTraversal_DoesNotWriteOutsideRoot` — `../../escape.txt` (the exact example from the finding), with explicit cleanup since the escape target is outside the temp content root.
   - `UploadAsync_AbsoluteFileName_DoesNotWriteOutsideRoot` — absolute/rooted filename, asserts no file at the absolute target.
   - `UploadAsync_EmptyOrWhitespaceFileName_ThrowsArgumentException` — empty and whitespace.
   - `UploadAsync_FileNameWithInvalidCharacters_ThrowsArgumentException` — NUL byte.
   - `UploadAsync_ParentReferenceFileName_ThrowsArgumentException` — bare `..`.
   - `UploadAsync_FileNameWithDirectoryComponent_StripsToLeafAndWritesUnderRoot` — positive test: `subdir/report.txt` is sanitized to `report.txt` under root.

All 14 UploadAsync tests pass (including the 7 new ones), and all 114 FileServiceTests pass. The fix is minimal, targeted, and follows existing code conventions (private helper with XML doc, same containment predicate as SafeResolve, same ArgumentException error pattern). No scope expansion, no dead code, no logic errors.

output.feedback:
The implementation correctly resolves the security finding using the preferred path described in the fix instructions. Verified against each requirement:

1. **Authentication/authorization registered** — `Program.cs` calls `builder.Services.AddAuthentication("Cookies").AddCookie()` followed by `builder.Services.AddAuthorization();` before `builder.Build()`. The chained `.AddCookie()` satisfies the 'Add...(scheme)' requirement so an authenticated principal can actually be established (not just 'AddAuthentication' alone, which would reject everyone).

2. **Pipeline middleware in correct order** — `app.UseAuthentication()` then `app.UseAuthorization()` are inserted between `UseStaticFiles()` and `MapControllers()`. This is the security-critical ordering: authn establishes the principal, authz consults it, and both run before endpoint routing resolves the controller action so the `[Authorize]` attribute is enforced on every `/api/files/*` request.

3. **Controller protected** — `FilesController` carries a class-level `[Authorize]` (correctly imported from `Microsoft.AspNetCore.Authorization`), covering browse/search/upload/download/delete/move/copy — i.e. the entire surface the finding calls out, including the read-only endpoints, not just mutations. No action carries `[AllowAnonymous]`.

4. **Documentation** — Both required locations are documented: a thorough `<remarks>` block on `FilesController` describing the class-level `[Authorize]` and the middleware ordering, and a dedicated 'Access Control' section in `README.md` that warns developers not to strip the attribute/middleware to 'fix' a 401, so the open-by-default behavior cannot be accidentally re-deployed.

5. **Tests** — Comprehensive new tests in `ProgramSecurityWiringTests.cs` (source-pattern assertions mirroring the existing `ProgramWiringTests` pattern) lock in presence, count, and end-to-end ordering of the middleware, plus `Controllers/FilesControllerAuthorizationTests.cs` (reflection-based) lock in the class-level `[Authorize]`, no `[AllowAnonymous]` opt-outs, the complete action set, and the XML-doc posture. A shared `SourceFileLoader` helper was extracted to avoid duplicating the project-root walk.

6. **Build + tests** — `dotnet build` is clean (0 warnings, 0 errors) and `dotnet test` passes 324/324.

**Minor scope note (not blocking):** The commit also consolidates the previously-duplicated multi-catch blocks in `Execute`/`ExecuteAsync` into a shared `TranslateKnownException` helper. This is a behavior-preserving DRY refactor — the same five exception types (ArgumentException, UnauthorizedAccessException, DirectoryNotFoundException, FileNotFoundException, IOException) are matched in the same order, `is`-type checks have identical subtype semantics to the prior `catch` clauses, `BadRequest(new {...})` and `new BadRequestObjectResult(new {...})` produce identical responses, and unknown exceptions still propagate via `throw;` (stack-trace-preserving). It is slightly beyond the strict security-task scope but improves code quality without changing behavior.

**Observation (WONTFIX — would expand scope):** The fix adds cookie authentication but the SPA frontend has no login flow, so its API calls will now receive 401/redirect-to-login. The fix instructions explicitly list auth as the preferred path, and the README documents this trade-off ('establish a session first through whatever login flow your hosting environment provides, or adjust the authentication configuration in Program.cs accordingly'). Adding a login UI is a separate feature beyond the security finding, so it is not flagged as an actionable issue.

No critical or minor issues to address.
