output.summary: Verified the two security commits (9159b7e path-traversal hardening, f5cf184 authn/authz gating) and the current uncommitted diff. The critical path-traversal finding is fully resolved: FileService.ResolveChildPath reduces the client-controlled file name to a bare leaf, rejects empty/invalid names, and re-applies the root-containment predicate before writing; the controller response also reports the sanitized leaf. The medium no-authentication finding is fully resolved: class-level [Authorize] plus Program.cs cookie authentication + authorization middleware wired before MapControllers. The current diff (test additions + comment updates) introduces no security regressions. One prior finding remains: the low-severity raw exception-message leakage was not addressed — TranslateKnownException still echoes ex.Message (including absolute server paths) in 400 responses. Re-reporting it at its original low severity.

findings.title: File-system exception messages (with absolute server paths) still echoed to clients
findings.description: TranslateKnownException (Controllers/FilesController.cs:251) returns `new BadRequestObjectResult(new { error = ex.Message })` for the well-known file-system fault types (ArgumentException, UnauthorizedAccessException, DirectoryNotFoundException, FileNotFoundException, IOException). The .NET Message property of these exception types embeds the absolute physical path involved (e.g. "Could not find file '/var/app/home/...'"), so every browse/read/write/delete/move error leaks the server's absolute home-root directory structure and OS to the client. This is the information-disclosure finding reported in the initial review; neither security commit (9159b7e, f5cf184) touched the exception-translation path, so it persists. Now that the API is authenticated the exposure is limited to authenticated callers, which is why this stays low, but defense-in-depth calls for not surfacing raw internals. Intended behavior: return a generic, stable error string (e.g. "Invalid path or file system error") to the client and log the full exception server-side instead. Note: the Upload-path 'Invalid file name' / 'Invalid path' ArgumentExceptions already use safe canned messages, so only the framework-generated messages are the concern.
findings.fixPrompt: In Controllers/FilesController.cs, change TranslateKnownException so it no longer echoes the raw exception text to the client. Currently (around line 251) it does `return new BadRequestObjectResult(new { error = ex.Message });`. Replace `ex.Message` with a generic, non-revealing message string such as `"Invalid path or file system error"` (do not interpolate any path, file name, or exception detail into the response body). Keep the BadRequestObjectResult(400) status and the same set of caught exception types so behavior/semantics are unchanged for callers. Optionally, log the original `ex` (with its Message and stack trace) via the injected logger at Warning/Error level so the detail is still available server-side. Do not change Execute/ExecuteAsync or any other call site. The goal is solely to stop leaking absolute server filesystem paths and OS-specific details to API clients.

findings.title: File-system exception messages (with absolute server paths) still echoed to clients
findings.description: TranslateKnownException (Controllers/FilesController.cs:251) returns `new BadRequestObjectResult(new { error = ex.Message })` for the well-known file-system fault types (ArgumentException, UnauthorizedAccessException, DirectoryNotFoundException, FileNotFoundException, IOException). The .NET Message property of these exception types embeds the absolute physical path involved (e.g. "Could not find file '/var/app/home/...'"), so every browse/read/write/delete/move error leaks the server's absolute home-root directory structure and OS to the client. This is the information-disclosure finding reported in the initial review; neither security commit (9159b7e, f5cf184) touched the exception-translation path, so it persists. Now that the API is authenticated the exposure is limited to authenticated callers, which is why this stays low, but defense-in-depth calls for not surfacing raw internals. Intended behavior: return a generic, stable error string (e.g. "Invalid path or file system error") to the client and log the full exception server-side instead. Note: the Upload-path 'Invalid file name' / 'Invalid path' ArgumentExceptions already use safe canned messages, so only the framework-generated messages are the concern.
findings.fixPrompt: In Controllers/FilesController.cs, change TranslateKnownException so it no longer echoes the raw exception text to the client. Currently (around line 251) it does `return new BadRequestObjectResult(new { error = ex.Message });`. Replace `ex.Message` with a generic, non-revealing message string such as `"Invalid path or file system error"` (do not interpolate any path, file name, or exception detail into the response body). Keep the BadRequestObjectResult(400) status and the same set of caught exception types so behavior/semantics are unchanged for callers. Optionally, log the original `ex` (with its Message and stack trace) via the injected logger at Warning/Error level so the detail is still available server-side. Do not change Execute/ExecuteAsync or any other call site. The goal is solely to stop leaking absolute server filesystem paths and OS-specific details to API clients.

output.summary: Verified the two security commits (9159b7e path-traversal hardening, f5cf184 authn/authz gating) and the current uncommitted diff. The critical path-traversal finding is fully resolved: FileService.ResolveChildPath reduces the client-controlled file name to a bare leaf, rejects empty/invalid names, and re-applies the root-containment predicate before writing; the controller response also reports the sanitized leaf. The medium no-authentication finding is fully resolved: class-level [Authorize] plus Program.cs cookie authentication + authorization middleware wired before MapControllers. The current diff (test additions + comment updates) introduces no security regressions. One prior finding remains: the low-severity raw exception-message leakage was not addressed — TranslateKnownException still echoes ex.Message (including absolute server paths) in 400 responses. Re-reporting it at its original low severity.

output.feedback: All three required comment rewrites are present in the working tree and persisted (verified by direct file reads after the diff). The changes are comment-only — grep of added lines confirms every `+` line is a comment (` *`, `//`) or blank; zero code/behavior changes. The Attempt 1 aria-label code regression is absent (the only aria-label in src/app.ts is the pre-existing closeBtn one at line 124). Verification results: (1) src/app.ts:41-44 — rewritten to state renderBrowse/renderSearch are re-exported so the per-domain test files under src/app/ (render-browse.test.ts, render-search.test.ts, render-orchestration.test.ts, start-app.test.ts) can import them from './app'; src/app.test.ts reference removed. (2) src/app.characterization.test.ts — header reframed to describe the CURRENT split (modules listed as existing, not upcoming); the 'Why this file exists alongside src/app.test.ts' framing removed; line 29 now 'Shared helpers (self-contained, with their own local mockResponse)'; line ~203 no longer says 'see app.test.ts'; lines ~210-216 now 'buildTable lives in dom-builders.ts ... the per-domain suites traverse' (future tense 'is being extracted' removed). The single remaining app.test.ts mention (line 9) is explicitly past-tense historical ('The old monolithic src/app.ts ... has already been split'), which the instructions permit. (3) src/api.test.ts — block (266-288) rewritten to state mockResponse is the shared helper imported from src/test-utils/mock-response.ts and pins UNION semantics; all inline-copy/app.test.ts/dedup-as-future references removed; null-body comment (367-368) now notes the delete endpoint relies on mockResponse({status:204, body:null}) mapping null→{}; text-only comment (381-382) now notes the shared helper parses text as JSON (union semantics). npx tsc --noEmit exits 0. npx vitest run shows 11 failed | 337 passed — IDENTICAL to the pre-existing baseline (verified by stashing the change and re-running: same 11 failed | 337 passed), so the fix introduces zero new failures. The 11 pre-existing failures are in upload-a11y.test.ts and related files testing aria-label attributes that are a separate incomplete feature, unrelated to this comment-only task.

output.summary: Reviewed the fixer's changes (stale-comment updates in src/app.ts, src/api.test.ts, src/app.characterization.test.ts, plus new FileServiceTests.cs characterization tests). The two previously-reported MEDIUM findings are both fully resolved: (1) the stale src/app.test.ts references and 'future refactor' framing were rewritten to describe the current shared-helper / per-domain-module reality, with api.test.ts now correctly stating mockResponse is imported from ./test-utils/mock-response; (2) the Execute/ExecuteAsync duplicated catch-clause DRY violation was eliminated by a single TranslateKnownException helper that both methods delegate to. The fix introduced no regressions and the new C# tests are well-formed. Two previously-reported LOW DRY findings remain unaddressed (error-formatting duplication across DOM modules, and the characterization test's divergent local mockResponse); re-reporting them below at low severity for an accurate record.

findings.title: 'Error: ' + (err instanceof Error ? ...) formatting duplicated across 3 sites
findings.description: The expression that renders a caught error to the status element — `'Error: ' + (err instanceof Error ? err.message : String(err))` — is duplicated verbatim at dom-builders.ts:165 and :168 (the async .catch path and the synchronous catch in makeActionButton), and again as an equivalent ternary at render-orchestrator.ts:88-90 (render's resultsEl.textContent). Any change to how errors are formatted for the user (trimming, prefix, fallback wording) must be applied in three places or the sync/async/orchestrator paths will drift. This was reported in the initial review and was not touched by this fix pass. Extract a tiny shared formatter (e.g. formatErrorMessage(err: unknown): string) and use it at all three sites.
findings.fixPrompt: In the TypeScript SPA, eliminate the duplicated error-to-string formatting. output.summary: I verified whether the previously-reported UI/UX findings were resolved. The current uncommitted diff only touches a C# test file (FileServiceTests.cs) with no UI surface, so it introduces no UI regressions. However, the fixer's attempt to resolve the high-severity Upload keyboard-accessibility finding consisted ONLY of adding RED/failing characterization tests (commit 5abeb16: src/app/upload-a11y.test.ts, edits to start-app.test.ts and app.css.test.ts) while explicitly NOT modifying production source. The commit message states 'Test-only changes (no production source touched)' and 'These currently FAIL (red) against the buggy code.' I confirmed src/app.ts:83 still has `uploadInput.hidden = true` and src/app.css still has no `.visually-hidden` or `:focus-within` rule, so the WCAG 2.1.1 keyboard failure is still live in the shipped UI. The other four prior findings (search input accessible name, loading indicator, empty state, Browse Files microcopy) were not addressed at all. All five findings are re-reported below.

findings.title: Upload control still unreachable via keyboard — fixer added only RED tests, not the source fix
findings.description: The prior high-severity finding was NOT resolved. The fixer's 'fix' (commit 5abeb16) added characterization tests (src/app/upload-a11y.test.ts, edits to src/app/start-app.test.ts and src/app/css.test.ts) that assert the CORRECT behavior, but the commit is explicitly 'Test-only changes (no production source touched)' and the tests 'currently FAIL (red) against the buggy code.' I verified src/app.ts:83 still reads `uploadInput.hidden = true;`, which sets the HTML `hidden` attribute → UA `display:none`, removing the input from the tab order. The visible affordance is a wrapping `<label class="btn">`, which is neither focusable nor keyboard-activatable on its own. Net effect: keyboard-only and screen-reader users still cannot reach or trigger Upload (WCAG 2.1.1 Keyboard failure), while mouse users can (label click forwards to the input). The accompanying CSS fixes were also never applied: src/app.css has no `.visually-hidden` rule and no `.toolbar label.btn:focus-within` rule. The tests the fixer added are correct in intent but are currently RED because the production code was never changed — the actual defect is still shipped.
findings.fixPrompt: Apply the production-source fix that the already-added tests (src/app/upload-a11y.test.ts, start-app.test.ts, app.css.test.ts) expect, so they turn green. In src/app.ts around lines 81-85, REMOVE the line `uploadInput.hidden = true;` and instead set `uploadInput.className = 'visually-hidden';` and `uploadInput.setAttribute('aria-label', 'Upload files');` (keep type='file' and multiple=true). The input must NOT carry the `hidden` attribute or a negative tabindex. Then in src/app.css ADD two rules: (1) a `.visually-hidden` sr-only clip pattern — `position:absolute; width:1px; height:1px; padding:0; margin:-1px; border:0; overflow:hidden; clip:rect(0,0,0,0); white-space:nowrap;` — which must NOT use `display:none` (that would re-introduce the exact bug); and (2) `.toolbar label.btn:focus-within` applying a visible, non-`none` focus indicator (e.g. `outline:2px solid currentColor; outline-offset:2px;`) so a sighted keyboard user sees focus land on the Upload button. After the change, run the vitest suite (src/app/upload-a11y.test.ts, src/app/start-app.test.ts, src/app/css.test.ts) and confirm all upload-a11y tests pass. Do not modify the tests; make the source satisfy them.

findings.title: Search input still has only a placeholder, no accessible name
findings.description: The prior medium finding was not addressed. src/app.ts:71-73 still constructs the search input with only `searchInput.type = 'text'` and `searchInput.placeholder = 'Search...'` — there is no associated `<label>`, `aria-label`, or `title`. A placeholder is not an accessible name: it is not reliably exposed to assistive technology and disappears once the user types, leaving the field unlabeled. Screen-reader users get no clear purpose for the field. This code is part of the modified src/app.ts and remains unchanged.
findings.fixPrompt: In src/app.ts where the search input is constructed (around lines 71-73), add an accessible name so the field is announced clearly by screen readers and remains labeled after the user types. The simplest fix: after `searchInput.placeholder = 'Search...';` add `searchInput.setAttribute('aria-label', 'Search files');`. Do not rely on the placeholder alone. Alternatively, add a visually-hidden `<label for>` element, but an aria-label is sufficient and consistent with the aria-label pattern used elsewhere (e.g. the dialog close button already sets aria-label). No test changes required, but if a test pins the input attributes, update it to expect the accessible name.

findings.title: Still no loading indicator while results are being fetched
findings.description: The prior medium finding was not addressed. In render() (src/app/render-orchestrator.ts), the results container is cleared synchronously (`resultsEl.innerHTML = '';`) and then the route data is awaited with no loading, skeleton, or 'Loading…' state in between. The status footer is intentionally left untouched to preserve navigation targets, so during the fetch the user sees an empty table and an empty/stale status with no indication that work is in progress. This hurts perceived performance and can look like a broken or empty UI, especially on slower connections or larger directories. The lifecycle is newly authored in render-orchestrator.ts and remains unchanged.
findings.fixPrompt: In src/app/render-orchestrator.ts render(), after clearing the results container (`resultsEl.innerHTML = '';`) and BEFORE the `await getApi().browse(...)` / `.search(...)` calls, render a lightweight loading placeholder into the results container so the user sees that work is in progress. For example, set `resultsEl.textContent = 'Loading…';` (or append a skeleton element with an appropriate class). Ensure the loading state is replaced when data arrives (renderBrowse/renderSearch already replace resultsEl contents) and also replaced in the catch block (which sets resultsEl.textContent to the error message). The render-race guard (`if (myToken !== renderToken) return;`) must still short-circuit superseded renders. Keep it minimal — a text node or simple element — and add a CSS class if you want a styled skeleton. Verify the loading state appears during a slow fetch and is cleared on success/error.

findings.title: Empty folders still render a bare table with no empty-state message
findings.description: The prior low finding was not addressed. renderBrowse() (src/app/render-browse.ts) builds the results table and appends rows; if `result.entries` is empty AND there is no parent '..' row, the table shows only the column headers with an empty body and no explanation. Users perceive a blank/missing listing rather than an explicitly empty folder. render-browse.ts is newly authored in this workflow and remains unchanged.
findings.fixPrompt: In src/app/render-browse.ts renderBrowse(), after appending the optional parent row and the entry rows to the tbody, if the tbody has no children (i.e. `result.parent === null` and `result.entries` is empty, so no rows were appended), render a clear empty-state message. For example: `if (tbody.children.length === 0) { const row = tbody.insertRow(); const cell = row.insertCell(); cell.textContent = 'This folder is empty.'; cell.colSpan = 4; }` (4 matches the Name|Size|Modified|Actions columns). Place this after the row-append loop and before `resultsEl.append(table);`. Verify an empty folder shows the message and a non-empty folder is unaffected.

findings.title: 'Browse Files' trigger still ambiguous next to an already-visible browser
findings.description: The prior low finding was not addressed. The page renders a fully interactive embedded file browser as the primary surface, and directly below it a button labeled 'Browse Files' (src/app.ts:103-105). Since files are already browsable inline, the button's purpose is unclear — a user may not realize it opens a second, dialog/modal view of the same browser. The microcopy remains unchanged.
findings.fixPrompt: In src/app.ts around lines 103-105 where the trigger button is created (`trigger.textContent = 'Browse Files';`), change the label to microcopy that communicates the button opens a pop-out/dialog version rather than implying it is the only way to browse. For example, use `trigger.textContent = 'Open in window';` or `trigger.textContent = 'Pop out file browser';`. Pick wording consistent with the project's tone and verify any test that pins the trigger text (search the test files for 'Browse Files') is updated to expect the new label.

The other four prior findings (search input accessible name, loading indicator, empty state, Browse Files microcopy) were not addressed at all. All five findings are re-reported below.

findings.title: Upload control still unreachable via keyboard — fixer added only RED tests, not the source fix
findings.description: The prior high-severity finding was NOT resolved. The fixer's 'fix' (commit 5abeb16) added characterization tests (src/app/upload-a11y.test.ts, edits to src/app/start-app.test.ts and src/app/css.test.ts) that assert the CORRECT behavior, but the commit is explicitly 'Test-only changes (no production source touched)' and the tests 'currently FAIL (red) against the buggy code.' I verified src/app.ts:83 still reads `uploadInput.hidden = true;`, which sets the HTML `hidden` attribute → UA `display:none`, removing the input from the tab order. The visible affordance is a wrapping `<label class="btn">`, which is neither focusable nor keyboard-activatable on its own. Net effect: keyboard-only and screen-reader users still cannot reach or trigger Upload (WCAG 2.1.1 Keyboard failure), while mouse users can (label click forwards to the input). The accompanying CSS fixes were also never applied: src/app.css has no `.visually-hidden` rule and no `.toolbar label.btn:focus-within` rule. The tests the fixer added are correct in intent but are currently RED because the production code was never changed — the actual defect is still shipped.
findings.fixPrompt: Apply the production-source fix that the already-added tests (src/app/upload-a11y.test.ts, start-app.test.ts, app.css.test.ts) expect, so they turn green. In src/app.ts around lines 81-85, REMOVE the line `uploadInput.hidden = true;` and instead set `uploadInput.className = 'visually-hidden';` and `uploadInput.setAttribute('aria-label', 'Upload files');` (keep type='file' and multiple=true). The input must NOT carry the `hidden` attribute or a negative tabindex. Then in src/app.css ADD two rules: (1) a `.visually-hidden` sr-only clip pattern — `position:absolute; width:1px; height:1px; padding:0; margin:-1px; border:0; overflow:hidden; clip:rect(0,0,0,0); white-space:nowrap;` — which must NOT use `display:none` (that would re-introduce the exact bug); and (2) `.toolbar label.btn:focus-within` applying a visible, non-`none` focus indicator (e.g. `outline:2px solid currentColor; outline-offset:2px;`) so a sighted keyboard user sees focus land on the Upload button. After the change, run the vitest suite (src/app/upload-a11y.test.ts, src/app/start-app.test.ts, src/app/css.test.ts) and confirm all upload-a11y tests pass. Do not modify the tests; make the source satisfy them.

findings.title: Search input still has only a placeholder, no accessible name
findings.description: The prior medium finding was not addressed. src/app.ts:71-73 still constructs the search input with only `searchInput.type = 'text'` and `searchInput.placeholder = 'Search...'` — there is no associated `<label>`, `aria-label`, or `title`. A placeholder is not an accessible name: it is not reliably exposed to assistive technology and disappears once the user types, leaving the field unlabeled. Screen-reader users get no clear purpose for the field. This code is part of the modified src/app.ts and remains unchanged.
findings.fixPrompt: In src/app.ts where the search input is constructed (around lines 71-73), add an accessible name so the field is announced clearly by screen readers and remains labeled after the user types. The simplest fix: after `searchInput.placeholder = 'Search...';` add `searchInput.setAttribute('aria-label', 'Search files');`. Do not rely on the placeholder alone. Alternatively, add a visually-hidden `<label for>` element, but an aria-label is sufficient and consistent with the aria-label pattern used elsewhere (e.g. the dialog close button already sets aria-label). No test changes required, but if a test pins the input attributes, update it to expect the accessible name.

findings.title: Still no loading indicator while results are being fetched
findings.description: The prior medium finding was not addressed. In render() (src/app/render-orchestrator.ts), the results container is cleared synchronously (`resultsEl.innerHTML = '';`) and then the route data is awaited with no loading, skeleton, or 'Loading…' state in between. The status footer is intentionally left untouched to preserve navigation targets, so during the fetch the user sees an empty table and an empty/stale status with no indication that work is in progress. This hurts perceived performance and can look like a broken or empty UI, especially on slower connections or larger directories. The lifecycle is newly authored in render-orchestrator.ts and remains unchanged.
findings.fixPrompt: In src/app/render-orchestrator.ts render(), after clearing the results container (`resultsEl.innerHTML = '';`) and BEFORE the `await getApi().browse(...)` / `.search(...)` calls, render a lightweight loading placeholder into the results container so the user sees that work is in progress. For example, set `resultsEl.textContent = 'Loading…';` (or append a skeleton element with an appropriate class). Ensure the loading state is replaced when data arrives (renderBrowse/renderSearch already replace resultsEl contents) and also replaced in the catch block (which sets resultsEl.textContent to the error message). The render-race guard (`if (myToken !== renderToken) return;`) must still short-circuit superseded renders. Keep it minimal — a text node or simple element — and add a CSS class if you want a styled skeleton. Verify the loading state appears during a slow fetch and is cleared on success/error.

findings.title: Empty folders still render a bare table with no empty-state message
findings.description: The prior low finding was not addressed. renderBrowse() (src/app/render-browse.ts) builds the results table and appends rows; if `result.entries` is empty AND there is no parent '..' row, the table shows only the column headers with an empty body and no explanation. Users perceive a blank/missing listing rather than an explicitly empty folder. render-browse.ts is newly authored in this workflow and remains unchanged.
findings.fixPrompt: In src/app/render-browse.ts renderBrowse(), after appending the optional parent row and the entry rows to the tbody, if the tbody has no children (i.e. `result.parent === null` and `result.entries` is empty, so no rows were appended), render a clear empty-state message. For example: `if (tbody.children.length === 0) { const row = tbody.insertRow(); const cell = row.insertCell(); cell.textContent = 'This folder is empty.'; cell.colSpan = 4; }` (4 matches the Name|Size|Modified|Actions columns). Place this after the row-append loop and before `resultsEl.append(table);`. Verify an empty folder shows the message and a non-empty folder is unaffected.

findings.title: 'Browse Files' trigger still ambiguous next to an already-visible browser
findings.description: The prior low finding was not addressed. The page renders a fully interactive embedded file browser as the primary surface, and directly below it a button labeled 'Browse Files' (src/app.ts:103-105). Since files are already browsable inline, the button's purpose is unclear — a user may not realize it opens a second, dialog/modal view of the same browser. The microcopy remains unchanged.
findings.fixPrompt: In src/app.ts around lines 103-105 where the trigger button is created (`trigger.textContent = 'Browse Files';`), change the label to microcopy that communicates the button opens a pop-out/dialog version rather than implying it is the only way to browse. For example, use `trigger.textContent = 'Open in window';` or `trigger.textContent = 'Pop out file browser';`. Pick wording consistent with the project's tone and verify any test that pins the trigger text (search the test files for 'Browse Files') is updated to expect the new label.

output.summary: I verified whether the previously-reported UI/UX findings were resolved. The current uncommitted diff only touches a C# test file (FileServiceTests.cs) with no UI surface, so it introduces no UI regressions. However, the fixer's attempt to resolve the high-severity Upload keyboard-accessibility finding consisted ONLY of adding RED/failing characterization tests (commit 5abeb16: src/app/upload-a11y.test.ts, edits to start-app.test.ts and app.css.test.ts) while explicitly NOT modifying production source. The commit message states 'Test-only changes (no production source touched)' and 'These currently FAIL (red) against the buggy code.' I confirmed src/app.ts:83 still has `uploadInput.hidden = true` and src/app.css still has no `.visually-hidden` or `:focus-within` rule, so the WCAG 2.1.1 keyboard failure is still live in the shipped UI. The other four prior findings (search input accessible name, loading indicator, empty state, Browse Files microcopy) were not addressed at all. All five findings are re-reported below.

findings.title: Upload control still unreachable via keyboard — fixer added only RED tests, not the source fix
findings.description: The prior high-severity finding was NOT resolved. The fixer's 'fix' (commit 5abeb16) added characterization tests (src/app/upload-a11y.test.ts, edits to src/app/start-app.test.ts and src/app/css.test.ts) that assert the CORRECT behavior, but the commit is explicitly 'Test-only changes (no production source touched)' and the tests 'currently FAIL (red) against the buggy code.' I verified src/app.ts:83 still reads `uploadInput.hidden = true;`, which sets the HTML `hidden` attribute → UA `display:none`, removing the input from the tab order. The visible affordance is a wrapping `<label class="btn">`, which is neither focusable nor keyboard-activatable on its own. Net effect: keyboard-only and screen-reader users still cannot reach or trigger Upload (WCAG 2.1.1 Keyboard failure), while mouse users can (label click forwards to the input). The accompanying CSS fixes were also never applied: src/app.css has no `.visually-hidden` rule and no `.toolbar label.btn:focus-within` rule. The tests the fixer added are correct in intent but are currently RED because the production code was never changed — the actual defect is still shipped.
findings.fixPrompt: Apply the production-source fix that the already-added tests (src/app/upload-a11y.test.ts, start-app.test.ts, app.css.test.ts) expect, so they turn green. In src/app.ts around lines 81-85, REMOVE the line `uploadInput.hidden = true;` and instead set `uploadInput.className = 'visually-hidden';` and `uploadInput.setAttribute('aria-label', 'Upload files');` (keep type='file' and multiple=true). The input must NOT carry the `hidden` attribute or a negative tabindex. Then in src/app.css ADD two rules: (1) a `.visually-hidden` sr-only clip pattern — `position:absolute; width:1px; height:1px; padding:0; margin:-1px; border:0; overflow:hidden; clip:rect(0,0,0,0); white-space:nowrap;` — which must NOT use `display:none` (that would re-introduce the exact bug); and (2) `.toolbar label.btn:focus-within` applying a visible, non-`none` focus indicator (e.g. `outline:2px solid currentColor; outline-offset:2px;`) so a sighted keyboard user sees focus land on the Upload button. After the change, run the vitest suite (src/app/upload-a11y.test.ts, src/app/start-app.test.ts, src/app/css.test.ts) and confirm all upload-a11y tests pass. Do not modify the tests; make the source satisfy them.

The finding was not addressed. The task explicitly required adding an accessible name to the search input in src/app.ts (around lines 71-73), but the file is unchanged: the input is still constructed with only `searchInput.type = 'text'` and `searchInput.placeholder = 'Search...'`. There is no `aria-label`, no `title`, and no associated `<label>` element. The upload input gets a visible `<label>` (uploadLabel wrapping it), but the search input is left with only a placeholder, which is not an accessible name and disappears once the user types. The fix is a one-line addition: `searchInput.setAttribute('aria-label', 'Search files');` immediately after the placeholder assignment, consistent with the existing aria-label pattern already used for the dialog close button (`closeBtn.setAttribute('aria-label', 'Close')`). No test pins these input attributes, so no test update is required. Please apply the targeted fix and re-submit.

The tests for the loading indicator are well-designed and cover the essential scenarios: verifying that a loading placeholder appears during browse and search fetches, that it is replaced on success and error, and that it re-appears when a new navigation starts a fetch. The use of fetch mocking to hold requests allows observation of the in-flight state, and the assertions are specific and meaningful. The tests follow existing project patterns, are independent, and effectively pin the expected behavior to catch regressions.

The loading indicator finding has been fully addressed in src/app/render-orchestrator.ts:render(). After synchronously clearing the results container (resultsEl.innerHTML = ''), the code now sets resultsEl.textContent = 'Loading…' before either await (browse/search), so during a slow fetch the user sees 'Loading…' instead of an empty/stale table. The placeholder is correctly replaced on every exit path: on success, renderBrowse/renderSearch both call resultsEl.innerHTML = '' and append the result table; on error, the catch block sets resultsEl.textContent to the error message. The render-race guard (if (myToken !== renderToken) return;) is intact after each await and still short-circuits superseded renders before they can clobber results or commit an error. The change is minimal, targeted, well-commented, and introduces no new patterns or dead code. No quality issues found.

The committed fix (7c7b947) is correct, minimal, and fully addresses every item from prior review rounds. The README.md Configuration section sentence (line 36) has been updated to exactly the required wording: 'The folder is created automatically on first use (lazily, the first time a file-system operation targets it), so a misconfigured path surfaces on the first request rather than at startup.' No source code (Controllers/FilesController.cs, src/app.ts, src/app.css) was modified. No unrelated README sections (Access Control, Limits) were added. No unrelated test files (FilesControllerAuthorizationTests, ProgramSecurityWiringTests) are present. The characterization tests are well-written, correctly scoped, and properly documented: ReadmeDocumentationTests guards the README wording against regression, the FileServiceTests lazy-init additions (Constructor_DoesNotCreateRootDirectory_BeforeFirstUse, Constructor_DoesNotCreateNestedHomeDirectory_BeforeFirstUse, Constructor_MisconfiguredHomeDirectory_DoesNotThrowUntilFirstUse) prove the lazy contract that the README documents, and SourceFileLoader is a clean, reusable helper. The tests correctly rely on the real lazy mechanism in Services/FileService.cs (the Lazy<string> _rootLazy materialized via the Root property / ResolveFullPath). One caveat: the working tree has uncommitted, out-of-scope changes (see issues) that should be reverted before the change is considered final, but the committed fix itself is sound.

issues.description: Uncommitted changes add an out-of-scope 'upload control keyboard accessibility' test block asserting a `.visually-hidden` sr-only CSS class exists in src/app.css. That class does NOT exist in src/app.css (grep finds nothing), so these tests would fail. This is a leftover artifact from a prior out-of-scope attempt that previous review rounds explicitly asked to be removed. These uncommitted changes are unrelated to the documentation fix and should be reverted (`git checkout -- src/app.css.test.ts`) to keep the change focused and the working tree clean.

Uncommitted changes rewrite the upload-control assertions to expect the input to NOT have the `hidden` attribute, to carry a `visually-hidden` class, and to expose `aria-label='Upload files'`. The unmodified source (src/app.ts line 83 still does `uploadInput.hidden = true;`) does not match any of these expectations, so the changed tests would fail. These are unrelated, out-of-scope, broken leftover changes from a prior attempt and should be reverted (`git checkout -- src/app/start-app.test.ts`).

output.summary: Verified the current diff (keyboard-a11y test additions in src/app.css.test.ts and src/app/start-app.test.ts) against the four prior findings. Finding home-folder-lazy is resolved in the working tree: README.md:36 now states the folder is created lazily on first use, not at startup, so I am not re-reporting it. The current diff itself only adds test code with thorough, accurate explanatory comments and introduces no new documentation issues. The remaining three prior findings (limits-undocumented, browse-response-incomplete, dev-diary-missing-entry) were NOT addressed by this diff and remain outstanding, so I re-report them with refreshed fixPrompts.

findings.title: Copy depth limit (32) and search result cap (500) are still undocumented in README
findings.description: Services/FileService.cs enforces two user-visible limits that the README never mentions. (1) MaxCopyDepth equals 32 (declared at FileService.cs:28): CopyDirectory aborts with IOException whose message is 'Directory copy depth limit exceeded (possible cycle)' once recursion reaches 32 levels (FileService.cs:380-382). A user copying a deeply nested tree gets an error they cannot explain from the docs. (2) MaxSearchResults equals 500 (declared at FileService.cs:20): Search silently stops collecting once it reaches 500 matches (FileService.cs:130, 152, 173), so a broad query can return fewer results than exist with no indication. Both are behavior-affecting and belong in the README so users understand truncated results and copy failures. This was not addressed by the current diff, which only modified test files.
findings.fixPrompt: In README.md, add a new 'Limits' section as a level-2 heading placed immediately after the Configuration section (i.e. after the line ending 'rather than at startup.' and before the '## Deep Linking' line). The new section content should be two bullet points. First bullet: state that Search results are capped at 500 — Search stops collecting matches once it reaches 500 results, so a broad query may return fewer entries than actually exist; suggest narrowing the query or scoping it to a subfolder. Second bullet: state that directory copy is limited to 32 levels of nesting — copying a tree nested more than 32 levels deep aborts with an IOException reading 'Directory copy depth limit exceeded (possible cycle)' to guard against stack overflow on pathological or self-referential directory trees. Do not alter any other section of README.md.

findings.title: Verification checklist omits path and parent from the browse response
findings.description: README.md line 64 (checklist item 5) states that GET /api/files/browse?path= returns JSON with entries, folderCount, fileCount, and totalSize. The actual response, defined by BrowseResultDto in Models/FileSystemModels.cs:7, also includes 'path' (the listed folder's relative path) and 'parent' (the normalized parent path, or null at the root). A reviewer following the checklist would think two fields are missing when the API is working correctly. This was not addressed by the current diff.
findings.fixPrompt: In README.md, update the Final End-to-End Verification Checklist item 5. The current text reads: 'GET /api/files/browse?path=' — returns JSON with 'entries', 'folderCount', 'fileCount', and 'totalSize'. Replace it with: 'GET /api/files/browse?path=' — returns JSON with 'path' (the listed folder's relative path), 'parent' (the normalized parent path, or null at the root), 'entries', 'folderCount', 'fileCount', and 'totalSize'. This matches the BrowseResultDto record at Models/FileSystemModels.cs:7 which has all six fields. Do not change any other checklist item.

findings.title: Development diary has no entry for the refactor, hardening, and accessibility work
findings.description: dev-diary.md currently has only two entries (harness generation; human review with embedded mode). This branch performed substantial additional work that is undocumented in the diary: splitting the src/app.ts monolith into focused modules under src/app/, restructuring the test suite into per-domain files, hardening FileService (lazy home-directory creation via Lazy of string, a 32-level copy-depth guard, idempotent delete, and TOCTOU elimination), refactoring FilesController with a shared Execute/ExecuteAsync fault translator, adding an ESLint flat config, and making the Upload control keyboard-accessible (WCAG 2.1.1) by switching from the hidden HTML attribute to a visually-hidden sr-only clip class with an aria-label and a focus-within focus ring on the wrapping label. The diary should reflect this work so the development history is accurate. This was not addressed by the current diff.
findings.fixPrompt: In dev-diary.md, append a third numbered entry after the existing two entries. The new entry number 3 should be titled 'Refactor & hardening pass' and summarize the work in one paragraph: splitting the src/app.ts monolith into focused modules under src/app/ and restructuring the test suite into per-domain files; hardening FileService with lazy home-directory creation (created on first file-system operation, not at startup), a 32-level copy-depth guard to prevent stack overflow on cyclic or deep trees, idempotent delete, and TOCTOU elimination; refactoring FilesController with a shared Execute/ExecuteAsync fault translator; adding an ESLint flat config; and making the Upload control keyboard-accessible (WCAG 2.1.1) by replacing the hidden HTML attribute (which ejected the input from the tab order via UA display:none) with a visually-hidden sr-only clip class, adding aria-label 'Upload files', and a focus-within focus ring on the wrapping label. Do not modify the existing two entries.

A reviewer following the checklist would think two fields are missing when the API is working correctly. This was not addressed by the current diff.
findings.fixPrompt: In README.md, update the Final End-to-End Verification Checklist item 5. The current text reads: 'GET /api/files/browse?path=' — returns JSON with 'entries', 'folderCount', 'fileCount', and 'totalSize'. Replace it with: 'GET /api/files/browse?path=' — returns JSON with 'path' (the listed folder's relative path), 'parent' (the normalized parent path, or null at the root), 'entries', 'folderCount', 'fileCount', and 'totalSize'. This matches the BrowseResultDto record at Models/FileSystemModels.cs:7 which has all six fields. Do not change any other checklist item.

findings.title: Development diary has no entry for the refactor, hardening, and accessibility work
findings.description: dev-diary.md currently has only two entries (harness generation; human review with embedded mode). This branch performed substantial additional work that is undocumented in the diary: splitting the src/app.ts monolith into focused modules under src/app/, restructuring the test suite into per-domain files, hardening FileService (lazy home-directory creation via Lazy of string, a 32-level copy-depth guard, idempotent delete, and TOCTOU elimination), refactoring FilesController with a shared Execute/ExecuteAsync fault translator, adding an ESLint flat config, and making the Upload control keyboard-accessible (WCAG 2.1.1) by switching from the hidden HTML attribute to a visually-hidden sr-only clip class with an aria-label and a focus-within focus ring on the wrapping label. The diary should reflect this work so the development history is accurate. This was not addressed by the current diff.
findings.fixPrompt: In dev-diary.md, append a third numbered entry after the existing two entries. The new entry number 3 should be titled 'Refactor & hardening pass' and summarize the work in one paragraph: splitting the src/app.ts monolith into focused modules under src/app/ and restructuring the test suite into per-domain files; hardening FileService with lazy home-directory creation (created on first file-system operation, not at startup), a 32-level copy-depth guard to prevent stack overflow on cyclic or deep trees, idempotent delete, and TOCTOU elimination; refactoring FilesController with a shared Execute/ExecuteAsync fault translator; adding an ESLint flat config; and making the Upload control keyboard-accessible (WCAG 2.1.1) by replacing the hidden HTML attribute (which ejected the input from the tab order via UA display:none) with a visually-hidden sr-only clip class, adding aria-label 'Upload files', and a focus-within focus ring on the wrapping label. Do not modify the existing two entries.

output.summary: Verified the current diff (keyboard-a11y test additions in src/app.css.test.ts and src/app/start-app.test.ts) against the four prior findings. Finding home-folder-lazy is resolved in the working tree: README.md:36 now states the folder is created lazily on first use, not at startup, so I am not re-reporting it. The current diff itself only adds test code with thorough, accurate explanatory comments and introduces no new documentation issues. The remaining three prior findings (limits-undocumented, browse-response-incomplete, dev-diary-missing-entry) were NOT addressed by this diff and remain outstanding, so I re-report them with refreshed fixPrompts.

findings.title: Copy depth limit (32) and search result cap (500) are still undocumented in README
findings.description: Services/FileService.cs enforces two user-visible limits that the README never mentions. (1) MaxCopyDepth equals 32 (declared at FileService.cs:28): CopyDirectory aborts with IOException whose message is 'Directory copy depth limit exceeded (possible cycle)' once recursion reaches 32 levels (FileService.cs:380-382). A user copying a deeply nested tree gets an error they cannot explain from the docs. (2) MaxSearchResults equals 500 (declared at FileService.cs:20): Search silently stops collecting once it reaches 500 matches (FileService.cs:130, 152, 173), so a broad query can return fewer results than exist with no indication. Both are behavior-affecting and belong in the README so users understand truncated results and copy failures. This was not addressed by the current diff, which only modified test files.
findings.fixPrompt: In README.md, add a new 'Limits' section as a level-2 heading placed immediately after the Configuration section (i.e. after the line ending 'rather than at startup.' and before the '## Deep Linking' line). The new section content should be two bullet points. First bullet: state that Search results are capped at 500 — Search stops collecting matches once it reaches 500 results, so a broad query may return fewer entries than actually exist; suggest narrowing the query or scoping it to a subfolder. Second bullet: state that directory copy is limited to 32 levels of nesting — copying a tree nested more than 32 levels deep aborts with an IOException reading 'Directory copy depth limit exceeded (possible cycle)' to guard against stack overflow on pathological or self-referential directory trees. Do not alter any other section of README.md.

findings.title: Verification checklist omits `path` and `parent` from the browse response
findings.description: README.md checklist item 5 (Final End-to-End Verification Checklist) states that `GET /api/files/browse?path=` returns JSON with `entries`, `folderCount`, `fileCount`, and `totalSize`. The actual response, defined by `BrowseResultDto` in Models/FileSystemModels.cs:7 (`public sealed record BrowseResultDto(string Path, string? Parent, IReadOnlyList<FileEntryDto> Entries, int FolderCount, int FileCount, long TotalSize)`), also includes `path` (the listed folder's relative path) and `parent` (the normalized parent path, or null at the root). This is mirrored in src/api.ts (`BrowseResult` interface lines 9-16). A reviewer following the checklist would incorrectly conclude two fields are missing when the API is working correctly. This was not addressed by the current diff (which only touched README's Limits section and test files).
findings.fixPrompt: In README.md, locate checklist item 5 under the '## Final End-to-End Verification Checklist' section, which currently reads: `5. **`GET /api/files/browse?path=`** — returns JSON with `entries`, `folderCount`, `fileCount`, and `totalSize`.` Update it to include the two missing fields so it reads: `5. **`GET /api/files/browse?path=`** — returns JSON with `path`, `parent`, `entries`, `folderCount`, `fileCount`, and `totalSize` (where `parent` is the normalized parent path or `null` at the root).` This matches the BrowseResultDto record in Models/FileSystemModels.cs and the BrowseResult interface in src/api.ts. Do not change any other checklist items.

findings.title: Development diary has no entry for the refactor, hardening, and accessibility work
findings.description: dev-diary.md currently has only two entries (harness generation; human review with embedded mode). This branch performed substantial additional work that is undocumented in the diary: splitting the src/app.ts monolith into focused modules under src/app/, restructuring the test suite into per-domain files, hardening FileService (lazy home-directory creation via Lazy<string>, a 32-level copy-depth guard with IOException, idempotent delete, and TOCTOU elimination), refactoring FilesController with a shared Execute/ExecuteAsync fault translator, adding an ESLint flat config, and making the Upload control keyboard-accessible (WCAG 2.1.1) by switching from the `hidden` HTML attribute to a visually-hidden sr-only clip class with an aria-label and a :focus-within focus ring on the wrapping label. The diary should reflect this work so the development history is accurate. This was not addressed by the current diff.
findings.fixPrompt: Append a new numbered entry (item 3) to dev-diary.md after the existing item 2, summarizing the work done on this branch. Match the existing diary's terse, prose style (no headings, just a numbered sentence/paragraph).

This branch performed substantial additional work that is undocumented in the diary: splitting the src/app.ts monolith into focused modules under src/app/, restructuring the test suite into per-domain files, hardening FileService (lazy home-directory creation via Lazy<string>, a 32-level copy-depth guard with IOException, idempotent delete, and TOCTOU elimination), refactoring FilesController with a shared Execute/ExecuteAsync fault translator, adding an ESLint flat config, and making the Upload control keyboard-accessible (WCAG 2.1.1) by switching from the `hidden` HTML attribute to a visually-hidden sr-only clip class with an aria-label and a :focus-within focus ring on the wrapping label. The diary should reflect this work so the development history is accurate. This was not addressed by the current diff.
findings.fixPrompt: Append a new numbered entry (item 3) to dev-diary.md after the existing item 2, summarizing the work done on this branch. The entry should be a concise paragraph covering: (1) splitting the src/app.ts monolith into focused modules under src/app/ and restructuring the test suite into per-domain files; (2) hardening FileService — lazy home-directory creation via Lazy<string>, a 32-level MaxCopyDepth guard that throws IOException, idempotent delete, and TOCTOU elimination; (3) refactoring FilesController with a shared Execute/ExecuteAsync fault translator; (4) adding an ESLint flat config; (5) making the Upload control keyboard-accessible (WCAG 2.1.1) by replacing the `hidden` HTML attribute with a visually-hidden sr-only clip class plus an aria-label and a :focus-within focus ring on the wrapping label. Match the existing diary's terse, prose style (no headings, just a numbered sentence/paragraph).

output.summary: Verified the current diff against the four prior findings. Two are now fully resolved: home-folder-lazy (confirmed in pass 1) and limits-undocumented (this diff adds an accurate README ## Limits section documenting the 500-result search cap and the 32-level copy depth limit with the exact IOException message — all values verified against Services/FileService.cs). The diff also adds comprehensive, well-commented documentation-accuracy tests in ReadmeDocumentationTests.cs and keyboard-a11y tests in the two .test.ts files; these introduce no new documentation issues. Two low-severity findings remain unaddressed by this diff: the verification checklist still omits the `path` and `parent` fields from the browse response shape, and dev-diary.md still has no entry for the refactor/hardening/accessibility work done on this branch.

findings.title: Verification checklist omits `path` and `parent` from the browse response
findings.description: README.md checklist item 5 (Final End-to-End Verification Checklist) states that `GET /api/files/browse?path=` returns JSON with `entries`, `folderCount`, `fileCount`, and `totalSize`. The actual response, defined by `BrowseResultDto` in Models/FileSystemModels.cs:7 (`public sealed record BrowseResultDto(string Path, string? Parent, IReadOnlyList<FileEntryDto> Entries, int FolderCount, int FileCount, long TotalSize)`), also includes `path` (the listed folder's relative path) and `parent` (the normalized parent path, or null at the root). This is mirrored in src/api.ts (`BrowseResult` interface lines 9-16). A reviewer following the checklist would incorrectly conclude two fields are missing when the API is working correctly. This was not addressed by the current diff (which only touched README's Limits section and test files).
findings.fixPrompt: In README.md, locate checklist item 5 under the '## Final End-to-End Verification Checklist' section, which currently reads: `5. **`GET /api/files/browse?path=`** — returns JSON with `entries`, `folderCount`, `fileCount`, and `totalSize`.` Update it to include the two missing fields so it reads: `5. **`GET /api/files/browse?path=`** — returns JSON with `path`, `parent`, `entries`, `folderCount`, `fileCount`, and `totalSize` (where `parent` is the normalized parent path or `null` at the root).` This matches the BrowseResultDto record in Models/FileSystemModels.cs and the BrowseResult interface in src/api.ts. Do not change any other checklist items.
